pages.text: 184-14  September 23, 2014  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Housing   Rehabilitation   Program   has   a  mortgage   against   certain   real   property   owned   by   Cynthia   Ventola   (the   “ Owner ” )   residing   at   6  Seaman   Road ,   Block   1 51.03 ,   Lot   1 0   (the   “ Property ” ),   in   the   amount   of   F ifteen   Thousand   Dollars  ($15,000 .00),   which   was   dated   August   29 ,   20 07   and   recorded   in   the   Essex   County   Register ’ s  Office on   September 20,   20 07   in   Book   1 2 088 , Page   7277   (the   “ WOHRP Mortgage ” ); and  WHEREAS,   there   is   a   first   mortgage   on   the   Property   from   Green   Tree   Servicing,   LLC  ( “ Green   Tree ” )   in   the   outstanding   amount   of   One   Hundred   and   Thirty-Two   Thousand,   One  Hundred   Thirty-Six   Dollars   and Forty Five Cents   ($ 1 32 , 136 . 45 ) (the   “ First Mortgage ” ); and  WHEREAS,   the   Owner s   desires   to   refinance   t he i r   home   and   obtain   a   new   mortgage  loan   from   Green   Tree   in   an   amount   not   to   exceed   One   Hundred   and   Thirty-Three   Thousand,  Five   Hundred   Dollars   ($133,500.00) ,   and   with   an   interest   rate   of   4.5 %   (the   “ New   Mortgage ” );  and  WHEREAS,   the New   Mortgage will pay off the First   Mortgage; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Owner   has   requested   that   the   Township   of   West   Orange   subordinate  the WOHRP Mortgage to the New Mortgage; and  WHEREAS,   based   on   the   amount   of   the   New   Mortgage   and   the   appraisal   accepted   by  Green   Tree ,   the   proposed   subordination   will   not   materially   change   the   equity   available   to   satisfy  the WOHRP Mortgage;  NOW   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Mayor   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   execute   a  Subordination   of   Mortgage   in   favor   of   Green   Tree   with   respect   to   the   Property   and   the   Owner s ;  and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Municipal   Clerk   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   attest   to   the  Mayor ’ s signature on the Subordination of Mortgage.  Karen J.   Carnevale   Honorable   Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted: September 23, 2014  4828-4612-8670, v. 1

documents.title: AUTHORIZING AMENDMENT TO LEASE AGREEMENT WITH VERIZON WIRELESS

pages.text: 185-14  September 23, 2014  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   on   or   about   July   22,   2012   a   resolution   was   passed   authorizing   the  execution   of   a   lease   agreement   between   New   York   SMSA   Limited   Partnership   d/b/a   Verizon  Wireless   ( “ Verizon ” )   and   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   (annexed   hereto   as  Attachment   “ A ” )   allowing   Verizon   to   utilize   approximately   sixty-four   (64)   square   feet   on   the   roof  of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   administrative   building   located   at   66   Main  Street   ( “ Town   Hall ” )   for   the   installation,   operation   and   maintenance   of   small   network   nodes,  antennas, wires, conduits and pipes; and  WHEREAS,   the   estimate   of   sixty-four   (64)   square   feet   was   in   error   and   the   actual   space  to be leased is actually approximately forty-five (45) square feet; and  WHEREAS ,   Verizon   and   the   Township   seeks   to   substitute   the   first   page   of   the   lease  agreement   with   a   substituted   first   page   reflecting   that   the   space   utilized   by   Verizon   will   be   forty-  five (45) square feet instead of sixty-four (64) square feet;  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   that   the   Mayor   shall   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   execute  his   initials   on   the   Amended   Page   1   of   the   L ease   A greement   annexed   hereto   as   Attachment   “ B ” ,  and the Municipal Clerk shall be and hereby is authorized to attest to the Mayor ’ s signature; and  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED ,   that   the   original   fully   executed   cop y   of   the   Amended  Page   1   of   the   L ease   A greement   shall   be   maintained   by   the   Municipal   Clerk   to   replace   the  original Page 1 of the Lease Agreement already maintained.  Karen J. Carnevale   Honorable Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted: September 23, 2014
Attachment   “A”
Attachment   “B”

SITE NAME: West Orange Mt Pleasant Ave SC SITE NUMBER: ATTY/DATE  Amended   Page   1   of   Lease   Agreement   (September   ,   2014)  Robert   D.   Paris,   Mayor   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   _________   (initial)  New   York   SMSA   Limited   Partnership   d/b/a   Verizon   Wireless   (initial)  LEASE   AGREEMENT  This   Lease   Agreement   (the   "Agreement")   made   this   day   of   July,   2014,   between   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   with   its   principal   offices   located   at   66   Main   Street,   West   Orange,   New  Jersey   07052,   hereinafter   designated   LESSOR   and   NEW   YORK   SMSA   LIMITED   PARTNERSHIP   d/b/a  Verizon   Wireless   with   its   principal   offices   at   One   Verizon   Way,   Mail   Stop   4AW100,   Basking   Ridge,   New  Jersey   07920   (telephone   number   866 ‐ 862 ‐ 4404),   hereinafter   designated   LESSEE.   LESSOR   and   LESSEE   are  at   times   collectively   referred   to   hereinafter   as   the   "Parties"   or   individually   as   the   "Party."  WITNESSETH  In   consideration   of   the   mutual   covenants   contained   herein   and   intending   to   be   legally   bound  hereby,   the   Parties   hereto   agree   as   follows:  1.   PREMISES.   LESSOR   hereby   leases   to   LESSEE   approximately   forty ‐ five   (45)   square   feet   on  the   roof   (the   “Rooftop   Space”)   of   the   building   (the   “Building”)   located   at   66   Main   Street,   Township   of  West   Orange,   County   of   Essex,   State   of   New   Jersey   (commonly   known   as   Lot   1   in   Block   67   on   the   tax  maps   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   Building   and   such   real   property   are   hereinafter   sometimes  collectively   referred   to   as   the   “Property”),   for   the   installation,   operation   and   maintenance   of  communications   equipment,   specifically   equipment   required   to   install,   operate   and   maintain   what   are  commonly   known   as   “small   network   nodes”;   together   with   such   additional   space   on   the   roof   of   the  Building   sufficient   for   the   installation,   operation   and   maintenance   of   antennas   (the   “Antenna   Space”);  together   with   such   additional   space   within   the   Building   and   on   the   roof   of   the   Building   for   the  installation,   operation   and   maintenance   of   wires,   cables,   conduits   and   pipes   (the   “Cabling   Space”)  running   between   and   among   the   Rooftop   Space   and   Antenna   Space   and   to   all   necessary   electrical   and  telephone   utility   sources   located   within   the   Building   or   on   the   Property;   together   with   the   non ‐ exclusive  right   of   ingress   and   egress   from   a   public   right ‐ of ‐ way,   seven   (7)   days   a   week,   twenty   four   (24)   hours   a  day,   over   the   Property   and   in   and   through   the   Building   to   and   from   the   Premises   (as   hereinafter  defined)   for   the   purpose   of   installation,   operation   and   maintenance   of   LESSEE’s   communications   facility,  subject   to   the   limitations   and   requirements   with   respect   to   access   set   forth   in   Paragraph   6   below.   The  Rooftop   Space,   Antenna   Space   and   Cabling   Space   are   hereinafter   collectively   referred   to   as   the  “Premises”   and   are   as   shown   on   Exhibit   “A”   attached   hereto   and   made   a   part   hereof.   In   the   event   there  are   not   sufficient   electric   and   telephone,   cable   or   fiber   utility   sources   located   within   the   Building   or   on  the   Property,   LESSOR   agrees   to   grant   LESSEE   or   the   local   utility   provider   the   right   to   install   such   utilities  on,   over   and/or   under   the   Property   and   through   the   Building   necessary   for   LESSEE   to   operate   its  communications   facility,   provided   the   location   of   such   utilities   shall   be   as   reasonably   designated   by  LESSOR.  2.   CONDITION   OF   PROPERTY.   LESSOR   shall   deliver   the   Premises   to   LESSEE   in   as   is  condition,   which   shall   be   ready   for   LESSEE’s   construction   of   its   improvements   and   clean   and   free   of  debris.   LESSOR   represents   and   warrants   to   LESSEE   that   as   of   the   Effective   Date   and   continuing  throughout   the   Term   (as   hereinafter   defined):   (a)   the   Building   (including   without   limitation   the   roof,  foundations,   exterior   walls,   interior   load   bearing   walls,   and   utility   systems)   is   (i)   in   good   condition,  structurally   sound,   and   free   of   any   leakage;   and   (ii)   the   Property   and   Building   are   in   compliance   with   all  Laws   (as   defined   in   Paragraph   23   below),   including   any   applicable   building   codes,   regulations,   or  ordinances   which   may   exist   with   regard   to   the   Building,   or   any   part   thereof;   and   (b)   the   Property   is   free  of   all   lead ‐ based   paint,   asbestos   or   other   hazardous   substances,   as   such   term   may   be   defined   under   any  applicable   federal,   state   or   local   law.   If   a   breach   of   the   representations   and   warranties   contained   in   this

2423-14  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING CHAPTER 1 3 , SECTION   4.1(a)(1)   TO UPDATE  THE TOWNSHIP ’ S   CONSTRUCTION PERMIT FEE   ORDINANCE  I.   PURPOSE  WHEREAS ,   th e   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   intends   to   update   its  Building   Sub code   Fee s   within   its   Construction   Permit   Fee   Ordinance   to   correct   a   long-standing  typographical error ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   Construction   Official   has   suggested   that   the   Building  Subcode Fee s   be updated as proposed below;  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New   Jersey,   that  Chapter   13,   Section   4.1(a)(1)   of   the   Township   Code   shall   be   and   hereby   is   re-affirmed   and   re-  adopted, and is hereby amended as follows :  II.   AMENDMENTS  13-4.1   Construction Permit Fee.  The   fee   for   a   construction   permit   shall   be   the   sum   of   the subcode fees   listed   in   paragraphs   a.  and b. hereof and shall be paid before the permit is issued.  a.   Building Subcode Fee.   The building subcode fee shall be:  1.   For   new   construction   and   additions,   $0. 0 25   per   cubic   foot   of   building   or   structure  volume,   provided   that   the   minimum   fee   shall   be   one   hundred   twenty-five   ($125.00)  dollars, and certificate of occupancy, fifty ($50.00) dollars.  III.   REPEAL OF CONFLICTING ORDINANCES  Any   Ordinances   of   the   Township   in   conflict   with   this   Ordinance   are   herby   repealed   to   the  extent of such conflict.  IV.   SEVERABILITY  If   any   part   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   deemed   invalid,   such   parts   shall   be   severed   and  the invalidity thereof shall not affect the remaining parts of this Ordinance.

V.   EFFECTIVE DATE  This   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   final   passage   and   publication   in   accordance   with  the law.  Honorable Susan McCartney   Honorable Robert D. Parisi  Council President   Mayor  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.  Municipal Clerk  Introduction: September 9, 2014  Adopted: September 23, 2014

LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This   legislation   is   intended   to   update   the   Township ’ s   Building   Subcode   Fees   within   its  Construction   Permit   Fee   Ordinance   to   correct   a   long-standing   typographical   error.   On   May   23,  2000,   Ordinance   No.   1703-00   was   adopted,   which   provided   that   the   Building   Subcode   Fee   for  new   construction   would   be   $0.018   per   cubic   foot   of   building   or   structure   volume.   On   April   26,  2005,   Ordinance   No.   2012-05   was   adopted,   which   increased   the   Building   Subcode   Fee   for   new  construction   to   $0.025   per   cubic   foot   of   building   or   structure   volume.   On   October   2,   2007,  Ordinance   No.   2146-07   was   adopted,   which   add ed   a   flat   fee   to   be   charged   for   retaining   walls,  shed   type   structures   and   swimming   pools,   and   declare d   that   the   maximum   fee   that   can   be  charged   for   obtaining   a   temporary   certificate   of   occupancy   is   $30.00,   in   accordance   with   State  regulations.   However,   in   Ordinance   No.   2146-07 ,   a   decimal   point   was   misplaced,   mistakenly  changing   the   Building   Subcode   Fee   for   new   construction   to   $0.25   per   cubic   foot   of   building   or  structure   volume,   increasing   that   fee   by   100   times.   T he   Construction   Official   has   stated   that   the  actual   fee   charged   has   been   $0.025   per   cubic   foot   of   building   or   structure   volume   since   he  started   working   for   the   Township   in   2006 .   It   has   recently   come   to   the   Construction   Official ’ s  attention   that   the   fee   listed   in   the   Ordinance   is   incorrect.   Thus,   the   proposed   ordinance   would  correct the typographical error.  4839-0767-7724, v. 1

2426 -14  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING CHAPTER   2, SECTION 17   OF THE  GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE , ENTITLED  “ DEPARTMENT OF R EC REATION AND CULTURAL AFFAIRS   . ”  BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST  ORANGE ,   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New   Jersey,   that   Chapter   4,   Section   33   of   the  Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   is   hereby   amended   and  supplemented as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  The   purpose   of   this   Ordinance   is   to   avoid   duplicating   the   prohibition   of   behavior   already  proscribed   in   criminal   statutes ,   to   revise   the   Township   park   ordinances   to   better   reflect  community   standards   of   acceptable   behavior ,   and   to   cultivate   a   more   peaceful,   family-friendly  environment in local parks.  II.   CHAPTER   2,   SECTION   17   SHALL   BE   AND   HEARBY   IS   AMENDED   AND  SUPERSEDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS :  2-17.1   Department Generally.  a.   There shall be a Department of Recreation and Cultural Affairs, the head of which shall  be the Director. Prior to his/her appointment, the Director shall have a bachelor's degree  from a recognized college or university, with specialized training in recreation or a related  field, and at least four (4) years of experience in public recreation administration. The  requirement of a bachelor's degree may be waived if a candidate for the position of  Director has at least fifteen (15) years experience and training in the field of public  recreation.  b.   At any time wherein the position of Director of Recreation and Cultural Affairs is vacant  the duties of the Director of Recreation and Cultural Affairs, in part or in total, may be  performed by a Deputy Department Head of Recreation. He/she shall receive such  compensation as may be fixed by ordinance.  Any references within this section to a Recreation Director shall mean the Director of  Recreation and Cultural Affairs or a Deputy Department Head of Recreation as the  Director of Recreation and Cultural Affairs.  (1972 Code § 2-12.1; Ord. No. 888-87 § 1; Ord. No. 2307-11 § II)  2-17.2   Residency.  The Director of Recreation shall be a bona fide resident of the Township. A bona fide resident,  for the purpose of this section, is a person having a permanent domicile within the Township  and one which has not been adopted with the intention of again taking up or claiming a previous  residence outside the Township limits. The Director need not be a full time resident at the time  of appointment but shall establish bona fide residence in the Township within one (1) year of  appointment or be dismissed from office. (1972 Code § 2-12.2; Ord. No. 888-87 § 2)

2-17.3   Advisory Committee; Program.  The Mayor shall appoint a Citizen's Advisory Committee on Recreation to advise and consult  with the Superintendent. With the aid and assistance of the Committee, the Director shall plan  and develop recreational facilities; promote and direct an all year program of cultural and leisure  time activities for children, youth and adults; provide for the optimum coordination and  development of public and private recreational facilities and programs. (1972 Code § 2-12.3)  2-17.4   Department; Functions.  The Department of Recreation and Cultural Affairs shall:  a.   Administer and operate parks, playgrounds and playfields and facilities for indoor and  outdoor sports, athletic and recreational programs and activities for children and adults.  b.   Sponsor and administer cultural and recreational programs and activities in cooperation  with other public and private agencies and organizations, and provide specialized  programs for the aged, the handicapped and other special purposes.  c.   Employ and instruct the Department of Public Works with respect to the maintenance and  repair of public buildings and grounds used, controlled or managed for recreational  purposes by the Division.  (1972 Code § 2-12.4; Ord. No. 363-75 § 1; Ord. No. 390-76  § 1; Ord. No. 478-78, A 1)  2-17.5   Regulations.  The following acts are expressly prohibited and/or regulated by the Recreation Department and  shall require enforcement under Chapter IV. The following such acts are prohibited and/or  regulated:  a.   Other than in regulated parking areas, no vehicle shall be allowed within any park owned  by the Township or under its jurisdiction at any time except for emergency vehicles or  others with written permission from the Recreation Director.  b.   It shall be strictly prohibited for one to start or maintain a fire of any kind or to use  barbecue equipment of any kind in any park owned by the Township or under its  jurisdiction or on other Township owned or maintained property.  c.   It shall be strictly prohibited for one to operate a powered flight model in any park owned  by the Township or under its jurisdiction or on other Township owned or maintained  property.  d.   It shall be unlawful for a person to enter into any park owned by the Township or under its  jurisdiction or on other Township owned or maintained property and post bills of any sort  without the express permission of the Recreation Director.  e.   A permit shall be required for organized group/team practices, games and/or activities. All  permits are issued through the Recreation Department. This shall include all recreational  facilities and open space located within the parks and playgrounds deemed to be under  the jurisdiction of the Recreation Department. The Recreation Department shall establish  reasonable guidelines, policies, and prohibitions for the issuance of said permits.  f.   The use of all recreational facilities under the jurisdiction of the Recreation Department  for financial gain by businesses, organizations, and individuals shall be prohibited without  the written permission of the Recreation Department.  g.   Scholastic games and practices shall have priority over all other users including those  who may have previously occupied the park area.  h.   Bike riding, roller blading, skateboarding and the use of any and all riding devices utilizing  wheels or rollers are prohibited on all hard court surfaces within any Recreation facility.

i.   People are prohibited from wearing hard sole shoes on the tennis courts within any  Township park. Additionally, the use of metal spiked or cleated footwear is prohibited on  all artificial or synthetic surfaces.  j.   There shall be one (1) hour time limit for the use of the tennis courts when other players  are waiting to use the tennis courts.  k.   The Recreation Department may charge and collect in advance a reasonable fee for  admission to or use of facilities, programs, or activities as specifically authorized by  Resolution of Council.  l .   Golfing activities are prohibited in all Recreation Department facilities.  m.   Abusive language and actions are prohibited in all Recreation Department facilities .  n.   The possession, use, and/or consumption of alcohol is prohibited in all Recreation  Department facilities without the written permission of the Recreation Director.  o.   Smoking of cigarettes and use of tobacco products within or adjacent to public parks,  playgrounds and ballfields under the jurisdiction of the Recreation Department is  prohibited.  p.   Excessive noise is prohibited at all times.  q.   All dogs must be leashed within the confines of all Recreation Department facilities.  Furthermore, dogs shall be prohibited from   being inside the confines of a K iddie   P ark , as  defined herein .  r.   No littering or dumping is permitted. Dumpsters located within all Recreation Department  facilities are restricted to municipal use only.  s.   All activities that endanger the health, safety or welfare of others, or that breach the  public peace, are prohibited at all times.  (1972 Code § 2-12.5; Ord. No. 1722-00 § 1)  2-17.6   Public Parks; Hours and Regulations.  a.   The following public parks of the Township, and the parking areas located within or  adjacent thereto, shall be closed to the public and for parking of any vehicles between the  hours of 10:00 p.m. and 6:00 a.m. prevailing time   unless otherwise posted .  1.   Byrne Memorial Park;  2.   Colgate Field;  3.   Degnan Memorial Field;  4.   Stagg Field;  5.   Lafayette Playground;  6.   William E. Boland Park;  7.   O'Connor Park;  8.   Jenkins Playground;  9.   Rolling Green Tennis Courts;  10.   Minish Park;  11.   Gilbert Place;

12.   Club Boulevard Playground;  13.   Ridgeway Avenue Park.  Any and all future parks under the jurisdiction of the Municipal government of the  Township of West Orange.  b.   It shall be unlawful for any person to enter in or upon, occupy, be present in or park any  vehicle, or suffer or permit to be parked any vehicle in the aforesaid public parks, or  parking areas located in or adjacent thereto, between the hours of 10:00 p.m. and 6:00  a.m., prevailing time, and it shall be unlawful for any person or persons to at any time  remove, damage, mutilate, deface or destroy any barricade, chain, notice, sign or other  object used to block the entrance to the aforesaid public parks or parking areas, or  indicating that the parks or parking areas are closed.  c.   Nothing herein contained shall be deemed or construed to apply to:  1.   Personnel of the Township engaged in or conducting Township business in any  public park or parking area.  2.   Any group or organization holding a permit to conduct an activity in such park or  parking area for a period specifically prohibited.  3.   Any person found in a park or parking area by reason of an unusual or unforeseen  emergency.  d.   There are areas within each park listed in subsection 2-17.6a, except for Rolling Green  Tennis Courts, that contain playground apparatus. These areas are considered "Kiddie  Parks." "Kiddie Parks" are to be used by children under the age of twelve (12) years old  and under the supervision of an adult.  e.   It shall be unlawful for any person, regardless of age, to enter in or upon, occupy, be  present in or park any vehicle, or suffer or permit to be parked any vehicle in any of the  "Kiddie Parks," or the parking area located in or adjacent thereto, after 10:00p.m.  (1972 Code § 2-12.6; Ord. No. 313-74, S 1; Res. 2416-76; New; Ord. No. 1722-00 § 2)  2-17.7   Use of Facilities by Non-Residents ; Rules and Regulations; Exceptions.  a.   Township owned playgrounds, fields and other recreation facilities shall only be utilized  by bona fide residents of West Orange.  b.   The Department of Recreation shall promulgate rules and regulations designed to carry  out the intent and purpose of this subsection.  c.   The provisions of this subsection shall not apply to any recreation facility wholly or  partially constructed or improved with New Jersey Green Acres trust funds.  (Ord. No. 1527-98)  2-17.8   Registration Policy for Participation in All Recreation Programs.  Each and every time a West Orange resident completes a registration form for  participation in a Township of West Orange Recreation Program, the resident must provide  documents to demonstrate (i) Parent/guardian proof of identity and residency (ii) Child's proof of  identity and school enrollment status.  a.   Proof of parent/guardian identity and residency by providing one of the following  documents:  1.   Valid New Jersey Division of Motor Vehicle Driver's License showing a West Orange  address;  2.   Valid New Jersey Division of Motor Vehicles ID Card showing a West Orange  address;  3.   Valid West Orange Recreation Department ID Card.

b.   Child's Documentation. Both of the following documents must be provided:  1.   Child's birth certificate; and  2.   Child's most recent report card.  c.   Proof of Custodial Situations.   Legal and binding court documents and/or a  correspondence or document signed by an attorney-at-law attesting to the custodial  situation shall constitute sufficient proof of the custodial situation.  (Ord. No. 2148-07 § II; Ord. No. 2219-09 § II)  2-17.9   Penalties  Any person or entity that violates any of the provisions of Sections 2-17.5   through 2-17.8  or any rule or regulation expounded pursuant hereto shall be subject to the replacement, repair  or restoration of any damaged park or recreation area property and shall be liable to a penalty  as follows:  a.   First offense: twenty-five dollars ($25).  b.   Second offense:   fifty   dollars ($ 50 ).  c.   Third offense:   one hundred   dollars ($ 100 ).  III.   REPEAL OF CONFLICTING ORDINANCES  Any Ordinances of the Township, which are in conflict with this Ordinance, are hereby  repealed to the extent of such conflict.  IV.   SEVERABILITY  If any part of this Ordinance shall be deemed invalid, such parts shall be severed and  the invalidity thereof shall not affect the remaining parts of this Ordinance.  V.   EFFECTIVE DATE  This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication in accordance with  the law.  Robert D. Parisi,   Mayor  Susan McCartney ,   Council President   Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk  Introduced: September 9, 2014  Adopted:   September   23 , 2014  Approved as to form on the basis of the facts provided:

2426-14  Legislative History  The   purpose   of   this   Ordinance   is   to   avoid   redundancies   between   regulations   found   in   the  Municipal   Code   and   proscriptions   already   contained   in   relevant   criminal   statutes.   Additional  purposes   for   revisions   include :   a   desire   to   have   the   regulations   reflect   shared   community  standards   of   satisfactory   behavior   and   to   cultivate   a   tranquil   environment   in   the   lo cal   parks.   In  order   to   prevent   redundancies   between   Municipal   Code   regulations   and   related   criminal  statutes ,   it   is   desired   to   eliminate   regulations   pertaining   to   vandalism   and   other   behaviors  already   outlawed   by   criminal   statute.   In   order   to   cultivate   a   more   tranquil   environment   in   the  local   parks,   it   is   necessary   to   explicitly   proscribe   excessive   noise,   abusive   language,   cigarette  smoking   and   other   analogous   behaviors   that   present   a   persistent   source   of   annoyance   or imperil  the   health,   safety,   and   welfare   of   residents.   The   Director   of   Recreation   and   the   Chief   of   Police  have both recommended the changes herein.  4811-0811-1900, v. 1

2427-14  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance creates three on street handicapped parking spaces, known as  Restricted Parking Spaces under N.J.S.A 39:4-197.5, to assist two residents with   D isabled  P ersons   I dentifications issued by the New Jersey Motor Vehicle Commission. One of the  spaces will be created on the westerly side of Longview Street, a public street within the Hutton  Lafayette Garden Apartment Complex, in the vicinity of number 63, and the other two spaces will  be created on Ashland Avenue just north of Park Drive in the vicinity of the apartment building at  31-33 Park Avenue where the other resident resides. These stalls will also serve the  Physician ’ s office at   35   Park Avenue.   Both these properties are corner lots with frontage along  Ashland Avenue.   These three stalls will not be Restricted   P arking Zones established under  Subsection 7.32.2 pursuant to N.J.S.A. 39:4-197.5 which are on street handicapped parking  stalls established in front of a residence for the exclusive use of   a resident of that home with a  D isabled   P ersons   I dentification. A complex of multifamily buildings prevents this on Longview  Street and the overnight parking prohibition on Park Avenue does not make it practical to place  such a stall there. These stalls once created, while assisting residen ts   of 63 Longview Street  and 31-33 Park Drive, will be available for anyone with a NJMVC   D isabled   P ersons   I dentification  with a corresponding special vehicle identification card.

2428-14  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance will prohibit left turns from Northfield Avenue eastbound onto Glen View  Drive   nor t h bound. Glen View Drive is located on the northerly side of Northfield Avenue just  east of the signalized intersection of Northfield Avenue and Old Short Hills Road. Due to the  presence of the traffic signal ,   westbound traffic along Northfield Avenue will often queue up to  and through the intersection of Northfield Avenue and Glen View Drive. Here Northfield Avenue  contains three travel lanes: two thru lanes and one exclusive left turn lane onto Old Short Hills  R oad. The volume of westbound traffic coupled with three lanes makes this left turn from  Northfield Avenue onto Glen View Drive difficult .   Vehicles waiting to turn onto Glen View Drive  from Northfield Avenue eastbound   block of one of the two eastbound lanes on Northfield  Avenue   which then delays eastbound traffic . This turn prohibition will result in the safe and  efficient movement of traffic. Residents of Glen View Drive will still be able to directly access  their homes from Northfield Avenue   we stbound. In the   ea stbound direction they will need to turn  left at Herbert Terrace, proceed to Beacon Place th e n Glen View Drive. From Old Short Hills  R oad they shall proceed westbound on Northfield Avenue to Herbert Terrace.

2429-14  L EGISLATIVE HISTORY  This   Ordinance   will   re s cind   A lternate   S ide   P arking   R egulations   on   Cary   Street   from  Valley   Road   to   the   Orange   Municipal   Line   and   allow   parking   on   both   sides   of   the   street   subject  to   restriction s   for   street   cleaning   and   maintenance.   This   will   provide   more   on   street   parking   on  Cary   Street   every   day   of   the   week.   Cary   Street   is   a   short   street   that   starts   at   Valley   Road   and  continues   into   the   City   of   Orange   approximately   185   feet   east   of  Valley   Road.   Most   of   the   parking   on   the   southerly   side   of   Cary   Street   will   be   opposite   the   area  on the northerly side where parking is prohibited by Statute within 50 feet of a stop sign.

pages.text: 161-14  September 9, 2014  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   there   is   certain   real   property   located   at   Block   1 67.03,   Lot   17   in   the  Township   of West Orange (the   “ Township ” ) , also known as   50 Haggerty Drive   ( “ Property ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   by   Deed   dated   June   12,   2013,   recorded   July   8,   2013,   the   Nordan   Realty  Corp.,   d/b/a   The   Reserve   at   Bel   Air,   820   Morris   Turnpike,   Suite   301,   Short   Hills ,   New   Jersey  07078   ( “ Nordan ” )   conveyed   the Property to the Township; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   desires   to   cancel   the   real   estate   taxes   on   the   Property   for   the  third   and   fourth   quarters   of   2013,   totaling   $ 4 , 104.68 ,   representing   the   taxes   due   after   the  Property was   transferred   to the Township ;   and  WHEREAS,   despite   having   conveyed   the   Property   to   the   Township,   Nordan   paid   taxes  on   the   Property   for   the   third   quarter   of   201 3   through   the   second   quarter   of   201 4   in   the   amount  of $ 8,095.59   to date; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township ’ s   Tax   Collector   has   stated   that   the   $ 8,095.59   in   overpaid  taxes should be   refunded to Nordan ;  NOW,   THERFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West Orange that :  1.   The   Township ’ s   Tax   Collector   and   Chief   Financial   Officer   be   and   hereby   are  authorized   to   issue   a   refund   to   Nordan   for   their   overpayment   of   real   estate   taxes  in the amount of $ 8,095.59; and  2.   That   the   remainder   of   the   real   estate   taxes   due   on   the   Property   for   2013   are  hereby canceled of record.  Susan McCartney   Karen J. pages.text: 165-14  September 9, 2014  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   The   Life   Christian   Church,   LLC   ( “ TLCC ” ),   a   non-profit   organization,   is   the  owner   of   property   located   at   747   Northfield   Avenue,   also   known   as   Block   166,   Lot   11.03,   on   the  Tax Map of the Township of West Orange (the   “ Property ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   TLCC   is   the   process   of   constructing   a   new   facility   upon   the   Property   (the  “ New Facility ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   on   October   1,   2013,   TLCC   and   the   Township   entered   into   an   Agreement  (the   “ Agreement ” ) ,   annexed   hereto   as   Attachment   “ A, ”   whereby   TLCC   agreed   various  conditions   including   completion   all   outstanding   exterior   work   no   later   than   April   15,   2015   in   order  to   obtain   a   Certification   of   Occupancy   perm itting   TLCC   to   occupy   a   limited   portion   of   the   New  Facility   in order   to house its administrative offices   during the pendency of construction ; and  WHEREAS ,   TLCC   now   intends   to   complete   the   second   floor   auditorium   and   related  space   before   the   third   floor   is   completed   so   as   to   more   fully   serve   its   membership   and   the  Township; and  WHEREAS ,   TLCC   has   requested   the   Township   extend   the   time   frame   for   completion   of  such outstanding exterior   until April 15, 2016 ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   is   amenable   to   extending   the   time   frame  consistent   with   the   terms   of   the   Amendment   No.   1   to   the   Agreement   By   and   Between   the   Life  Christian   Church   and   the   Township   of   West   Orange   ( “ Amendment   No.   1 ” )   as   set   forth   in  Attachment   “ B, ”   annexed hereto , and  WHEREAS ,   TLCC   has   confirmed   its   agreement   with   Amendment   No.   1   through  execution of same;  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE   that   the   Mayor   shall   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   and  empowered   to   execute   Amendment   No.   1,   annexed   hereto   as   Attachment   “ B, ”   and   that   the

Municipal Clerk shall be and hereby is authorized to attest to the Mayor ’ s signature; and  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED ,   that   the   original   fully   executed   copies   of   Attachment   “ B ”  shall be maintained by the Municipal Clerk with a copy to be provided to TLCC.  Karen   J.   Carnevale   Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   September 9, 2014  4813-0418-7421, v. 1

documents.title: AUTHORIZING REMOVAL OF SIDEWALK IN FRONT OF 34 STANFORD AVENUE

pages.text: 166-14  September 9, 2014  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS   ,   a section of the sidewalk in front of the property located at 34 Stanford Avenue (the  “ Property ” ) within the Township of West Orange has fallen into a state of disrepair as shown in  the pictures annexed hereto as Attachment   “ 1; ”   and  WHEREAS   , the sidewalk in front of the Property ends abruptly and does not appear to lead to or  connect with any other sidewalk or walking surface; and  WHEREAS   ,   both the Township of West Orange   Municipal   Engineer and   the West Orange  Police Chief   have visited the Property, observed the condition of the sidewalk in front of the  property, and   agree that there is no need or purpose for this section of the sidewalk to remain in  place   as it is unlikely that the sidewalk will be extended ;   and  WHEREAS,   the Township of West Orange shall reserve its right to require re-installation of the  sidewalk at a later time if deemed necessary by the Township of West Orange or its officials;  NOW, BE IT HEREBY RESOLVED   BY THE TOWNSHIP COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP OF  WEST ORANGE that the   owner of the Property is authorized to remove the section of the  sidewalk in front of the Property that is adjacent to the   western   section of the   Property   that runs  from the western edge of the walkway to the doorway of the house to the Property ’ s western  boundary as depicted Attachment   “ 1 ”   under the following conditions: (1) that the owner of the  Property nor any subsequent owner(s) of the Property do not degrade the right of way behind  the curb or install any improvements that would interfere with the future re-installation of a  sidewalk if deemed necessary by the Township, (2) that the   current   owner of the Property obtain  all necessary permits prior to commencement the removal of the sidewalk, and (3)   that the  sidewalk be replaced with grass to maintain an aesthetic consistent with the other propert ies  neighboring the Property.  Karen   J.   Carnevale   Honorable Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   September 9, 2014  4811-6463-6957, v. 1

The   Clerk   of   the   Township   is   hereby   directed   to   cause   a   copy   of   this  resolution to be filed with the Local Finance Board as a part of such application.  RECORDED VOTE :  AYE   NO   ABSTAIN  ABSENT  Karen J. Carnevale   Susan McCartney  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: September 9, 2014

168-14  September 9, 2014  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS,   the Township of West Orange entered into a contract with Conquest  Industries, Inc. having its principal place of business at 32 Woodland Avenue, Westwood, New  Jersey for Downtown West Orange Streetscape and Traffic Improvements in the amount of  $1,549,696.25 as awarded by a Resolution of the Township Council No. 50-11 dated March 8,  2011 for the base bid and two alternate bids;  WHEREAS,   these improvements are funded by a Federal Earmark Grant in the amount  of $2,015,777 which is administered by the New Jersey Department of Transportation (NJDOT);  and  WHEREAS,   the Township previously approved five NJDOT Change Orders in the  amounts of $120,000.00, $74,455.90, $0.00, $113,947.13 and -$9,117.35 which increased the  contract amount to $1,848,981.23; and  WHEREAS,   during the course of construction numerous field conditions were  encountered which required field changes to the plan; and  WHEREAS,   the field conditions required adjustments in curb location and construction,  inlet reconstruction, building foundation reconstruction, modification of electric junction boxes,  additional test pits for traffic signals, traffic signal modification, crosswalk relocation, landscaping  and in some locations additional site clearing; and  WHEREAS,   the field conditions and as-built quantities for Item No. ’ s 5, 22, 23, 54, 62,  63 and 65 which were for various traffic markings, traffic striping and landscaping resulted in a  net decrease of $4,160.20 on Change Order No. 6; an  WHEREAS,   on Change Order No. 6 an additional 250.75 man hours of Uniform Traffic  Directors were added at a cost of $15,045 bringing the total increase associated with Change  Order No. 6 to $10,884.80; and

WHEREAS,   the Township Engineer and its consultant Maser Consulting (successors to  Birdsall Services Group) recommend Change Order No. 6 as being necessary and beneficial  which is a net increase of $10,884.80; and  WHEREAS,   the Change Order reflects a 0.59 percent increase in the previously  authorized contract amount increasing the authorized contract amount to $1,859,866.73 or a 20  percent increase over the award amount of $1,549,696.25; and  WHEREAS,   sufficient funds exist to pay for the Change Order; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs in the foregoing.  NOW, THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of  West Orange that Change Order No. 6 in the amount of $10,884.80 with Conquest Industries,  Inc. for Downtown West Orange Streetscape and Traffic Improvements, increasing the  authorized contract amount to $1,859,866.73 is hereby approved and the Township Council  further authorizes Robert D. Parisi, Mayor to execute said Change Orders.  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney, Council  President  I hereby certify funds are available from:   ACCOUNT NO.: 03-2227-22-2100-103  John Gross, Chief Financial Officer

pages.text: 169-14  September 9, 2014  RESOLUTION TO AUTHORIZE AN AWARD TO EASTERN COMMUNICATIONS, INC. FOR  THE INSTALLATION OF A DIGITAL MICROWAVE COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  WHEREAS   , the Township of West Orange   “ Township ”   is responsible for the maintenance and  ongoing upgrades to the   Harris   800   MHz   Simulcast Public Safety EDACS Radio System, and  WHEREAS   , the purpose of this equipment is to provide   e mergency personnel   a separate   radio  bandwidth to communicate ; and  WHEREAS   , this equipment is housed in three locations :   West Orange Police Department  Radio Room Control Point, Marcella Avenue Communications Tower, and Chetwynd Terrace  Communications Tower; and  WHEREAS,   currently there are multiple leases with the local telephone company for T-1 circuits  to provide these services; and  WHEREAS   , converting for T-1 circuits to   Ethernet   conductivity is the most cost efficient and a  scalable system for future upgrades; and  WHERAS,   the Township did advertise for bids according to Local Public Contracts   N.J.S.A.  40 A:11-23   and did receive three (3) bids at the date and time   designated   (Exhibit A, attached) ;  and  WHEREAS   , Eastern Communications, Ltd has proposed a turnkey implementation including  interface to the Township ’ s existing Harris 800   MHz   EDACS trucked radio system . EDACS  technology is proprietary to Harris Corporation requiring technical support only from authorized  service agencies to insure the integrity and warranty of the Township ’ s system; and  WHEREAS,   after technical review is have been recommended that Eastern Communications,  Inc.   has  submitted a responsible and responsive proposal that meets all the requirements set forth in the  specifications at the lowest cost.  NOW BE IT RESOLVED   by the Council of the Township of West Orange that Eastern  Communications, Ltd., 48-14 36 th   Street, Long Island City, New York 11101 be awarded  contract to Install a Digital Microwave Communication System in the amount of $62,534.83.  BE IT FUTHER RESOLVED   that   a purchase order will be issued and executed by the  Township ’ s Purchasing Agent with funds certified by the Chief Financial Officer.  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney, Council  President  Adopted: September 9, 2014  I hereby certify funds available in account   :   03-2407-14-0030-030  John O. Gross, Chief Financial Officer

171-14  September 9, 2014  HISTORIC PRESERVATION CERTIFIED LOCAL GOVERNMENT  GRANT  Resolution Authoring Award for Historical Architectural Services  WHEREAS, the Township of West Orange   “ Township ”   had applied for   a   Certified Local Grant  “ CLG ”   and was approved to receive the amount of $24,500.00; and  WHEREAS,   the se funds afford the Township   the   opportunity to participate more directly in State  and Federal Historic Preservation Programs; and  WHEREAS, the goals   of the CLG   are to identify all buildings, sites, st r u c tures, objects and  districts that are significant in American history, architecture, engineering, etc.   within the  Township , that meet criteria for inclusion in the National Register of Historic Places and  implement planning tools for protection of these resources ;   and  WHEREAS , the   Township has advertised a Request for Proposal for a Historic Architect to  collect all the necessary information to satisfy the terms of the CLG, and  WHEREAS, on the date and place advertised did receive three (3) proposals   (attached Exhibit  A) ; and  WHEREAS, after review by the West Orange Planning Board ,   Hunter Research, Inc was  selected to   perform the necessary documentation and reports; and  WHEREAS, the cost of these servic es will be initially paid by the Township and that cost will be  reimbursed as per the terms of the CLG.  NOW BE IT RESOLVED by the Township of West Orange Council that the Hunter Research  Group, 120 West State Street, Trenton, New Jersey 08608 be awarded the contract to conduct  the study of the Historic Preservation Sites in accordance with the CLG funds awarded the  Township.  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney, Council  President  Adopted: September 9, 2014

pages.text: 172-14  September 9, 2014  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )  recognizes   that,   according   to   the   Substance   Abuse   and   Mental   Health   Services   Administration  ( “ SAMHSA ” ), one in five Americans have experienced   issues with   mental illness; and  WHEREAS,   despite   major   accomplishments   and   the   progress   achieved,   New   Jersey  must   still   overcome   significant   challenges   before   it   can   reach   the   goal   of   making   quality   mental  health care available to all those who need it; and  WHEREAS,   according   to   SAMHSA,   mental   health   problems   are   more   common   than  cancer   and   heart   disease   combined,   and   according   to   the   Veteran ’ s   Administration   ( “ VA ” ),   more  than   half   of   all   Iraq   and   Afghanistan   veterans   hospitalized   at   VA   hospitals   are   treat ed   for   mental  illness, and the stigma associated with mental illness is the biggest roadblock to recovery; and  WHEREAS,   the   Codey   Fund   for   Mental   Health   has   initiated   a   statewide   ca mpaign  requesting   towns   throughout   New   Jersey   to   designate   their   municipality   a   Stigma   Free   Town;  and  WHEREAS,   by   declaring   West   Orange   a   Stigma   Free   Town,   West   Orange   supports   the  elimination   of   any   stigma   associated   with   mental   health   illness   and   will   promote   public  awareness   of   mental   health   issues   and   the   removal   of   any   barriers   to   recovery,   and   work   to  overcome stigma, stereotyping and discrimination .  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange   that   the Township of West Orange is a Stigma Free Town.  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   on   major   thoroughfares   in   and   out   of   the   municipality,  road signs be erected showing the establishment of a Stigma Free Town.  Karen J. Carnevale,   Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney ,   Council President  Adopted:   September 9, 2014  4828-0515-5102, v. 1

pages.text: 174-14  September 9, 2014  RESOLUTION TO AUTHORIZE FINANCIAL ADVISORY SERVICES  WHEREAS   , the Township of West Orange   “ Township ”   has used NW Financial Services in the  past for various   financial   matters; and  WHEREAS   , there have been Federal and State regulatory changes that effect   the Township ’ s  issuance of general obligation notes and bonds; and  WHEREAS   ,   NW Financial Advisory Services has responded to the Township ’ s Request for  Qualifications pursuant to the fair and open process for the year 2014; and  WHEREAS   , pursuant to N.J.S.A. 40A:11-5(1)(a)(i), the proposed professional service contract  is subject to an exception to the standard bidding procedures set forth in the Local Public  Contracts Law ; and  WHEREAS,   attached is a list of rates (Exhibit   “ A ” ) for services to be provided.  NOW, BE IT HEREBY RESOLVED   , by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange  that the Mayor be and is hereby authorized to execute an agreement,   to NW Financial Advisory  Services,  2 Hudson Place, 3 rd   Floor, Hoboken, NJ 07030,   on terms and conditions set forth in the  agreement and the Municipal Clerk shall be and hereby is authorized to attest to the Mayor ’ s  signature.
