Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney, Council  President  Adopted: September 9, 2014

175-14  September 9, 2014  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS,   by Resolution No. 126-12, dated June 26, 2013, the Township Council  awarded a contract to Sanitary Construction Company, 415 Franklin Avenue, Rockaway, New  Jersey 07866 in the amount of $3,483,873 for Llewellyn Park Roadway and Sanitary Sewer  Improvements; and  WHEREAS,   these improvements were being made pursuant to Ordinance No. 2323-11  which authorized the Township to make the improvements and assess the benefitting property  owners for their cost; and  WHEREAS,   the Township by Ordinance No. 2358-12 appropriated $4,184,305.00 for  this project of which $500,000 consisted of a cash deposit by the Llewellyn Park Committee of  Managers; and  WHEREAS,   the Township by Resolution No. 165-13 dated August 20, 2013 at the  request of the Llewellyn Park Committee of Mangers authorized additional funds in the amount  of $348,387.00 by approving Change Order No. 1; and  WHEREAS,   this additional funding was used to address additional costs associated with  unforeseen field conditions and it increased the authorized funding for this contract to  $3,832,260.00 or a ten percent increase over the original contract award amount; and  WHEREAS,   as the work continued more field conditions needed to be addressed and as  of August 2014 the total amount expended for this project was $3,827,593.70 but several  roadway and sewer improvements specified in the original contract bid documents and  specifications still needed to be completed; and  WHEREAS,   by Ordinance No. 2409-14 adopted   May   6 , 2014   the Township appropriated  an additional $$550,000 for Llewellyn Park Roadway and Sanitary Sewer Improvements; and  WHEREAS,   in August 2014 Carl Jenne of Jenne Associates, LLC the project ’ s designer  and contract administrator and inspector for the Township and John Bober of the Llewellyn Park  Committee of Managers negotiated a lump sum price of $500,000 to complete the remaining

improvements plus an allowance of $45,000 for unit price and contingency items as   depicted on  Construction Drawings P-1 and S-4 prepared by Jenne Associates and revised July 17, 2014,  described by Mr. Jenne in his memo of August 25, 2014   and as   stated by Sanitary Construction  Company, Inc., in their proposal of August 15, 2014  WHEREAS,   Mr. Jenne estimated the cost of this additional work is about ten percent  less than it would be based on unit prices bid in the contract; and  WHEREAS,   the Llewellyn Park Committee of Managers by letter dated August 4, 2013  requested the Township authorize Change Order No. 2 with Sanitary Construction Company,  Inc., in the amount of $545,000 to complete the improvements specified in the original bid  documents; and  WHEREAS,   the additional funding increases the total authorized funding for this contract  to $4,377,260.00 or a 25.6 percent increase over the original award amount.; and  WHEREAS,   the State ’ s Administrative Code specifies procedures for authorizing  Change Orders in excess of 20 percent which includes a statement by the contracting unit ’ s  engineer; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer by letter dated September 3, 2014 recommended  Change Order No.   2   with Sanitary Construction Company, Inc. in which he stated:     This Change Order is necessitated by the unforeseen field conditions in Llewellyn Park,  one of the Country ’ s older planned communities   which   caused additional expenditures  as the project progressed.     The additional appropriation associated with this contract is to complete roadway and  sewer improvements that were specified in the original documents, and it does not  represent an expansion in the scope of the project.     The costs for this additional work were negotiated on a lump sum basis, and it  represents a reduction from the estimate cost of the improvements if the unit prices bid  were used.     To award as a separate contract for this work would likely result in higher costs as  Sanitary Construction Company, Inc. can achieve economies of scale as a result of their  presence in Llewellyn Park.  ; and  WHEREAS,   sufficient funds exist for this Change Order; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs in the foregoing.

NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of  West Orange that Change Order No. 2 for the contract Llewellyn Park Roadway and Sanitary  Sewer Improvements between Sanitary Construction Company, 415 Franklin Avenue,  Rockaway, New Jersey 07866 and the Township of West Orange which authorizes additional  funding in the amount of $545,000 to complete all improvements as originally specified in the bid  documents and plans which brings the total amount authorized through Change Order No. 2 for  this project to $4,377,260 or a 25.64 percent increase from the original contract amount is  approved.  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that the Mayor and Municipal Engineer are authorized to  sign the Change Order  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that in accordance with the State ’ s Administrative Code a  notice of the Change Order shall be published in the West Orange Chronicle.  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, MUNICIPAL CLERK   SUSAN McCARTNEY   , COUNCIL  PRESIDENT  Adopted: September 9, 2014  I hereby Certify funds are available from:   Account No.  03-2358-12-0010-010 $76,791.51  03-2358-12-0010-020 $468,208.49  JOHN GROSS, CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER

176-14  September 9, 2014  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   certain   property   located   at   293   St.   Cloud   Avenue ,   Block   168,   Lot   15.03   (the  “ Property ” )   in   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   " Township ")   was   subject   to   various   complaints  for   property   maintenance   and   other   violations   before   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Municipal  Court   (the   “ Municipal Court ” )   in 2010 and 2011 ; and  WHEREAS ,   on   May   20,   2010,   the   Township   issued   to   the   P roperty   owner,   Bruce   H.  Brumlop   ( “ Mr.   Brumlop ” ),   Summons   030588   for   failure   to   maintain   the   exterior   of   the   premises  at the Property (the   “ Summons ” );   and  WHEREAS ,   on   September   29,   2010,   Mr.   Brumlop   pled   guilty   to   the   Summons,   paid   a  fine of $183, and committed to paint the home by October 19, 2010;   and  WHEREAS ,   on   August   3,   2011,   having   not   painted   the   home,   Mr.   Brumlop   appeared   in  Municipal   Court   after   missing   six   (6)   prior   hearings ,   and   commited   to   retaining   a   contractor   to  paint the home; and  WHEREAS ,   on   August   16,   2011,   Mr.   Brumlop   made   his   final   appearance   in   Municipal  Court, and the home on the Property had   still   not been painted; and  WHEREAS ,   thereafter,   Mr.   Brumlop   did   not   appear   for   required   court   dates   on   August  30,   2011,   September   14,   2011,   October   26,   2011   and   February   22,   2012 ,   while   the   conditions  at the Property worsened; and  WHEREAS ,   on   November   15,   2011,   the   Honorable   Harry   L.   Starrett,   Chief   Judge   of   the  Municipal   Court,   entered   an   “ Order   Authorizing   the   Township   of   West   Or ange   to   Enter   Onto  Property   to   Partially   Abate   Prop erty   Maintenance   Code   Violation, ”   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit  “ A, ”   which,   among   other   things:   (i)   authorized   the   Township   or   a   third-party   approved   by   the  Township   to   enter   on   to   the   Property   to   perform   work   to   abate   said   violations,   if   they   were   not  yet   resolved;   and   (ii)   ordered   that   the   Township   could   record   a   lien   on   the   Property ,   without  further   notice   to   Defendant,   “ for   the   amount   of   any   and   all   services   rendered   to   abate ”   the  violations, and collect statutorily-authorized interest on that lien; and

WHEREAS ,   on   January   3,   2012,   Gerhard   Huegel,   the n   the   Township ’ s   Senior   Architect,  submitted   a   Certification   to   the   Municipal   Court   indicating   that   he   inspected   the   exterior  conditions   of   the   Property,   and   found   severe   and   urgent   conditions   necessitating   inspection   of  the   interior   of   the   residence   at   the   Property   in   a   further   effort   to   remediate   the   violating  conditions; and  WHEREAS ,   on   January   5,   2012,   the   Municipal   Court   entered   an   Order ,   annexed   hereto  as   Exhibit   “ B, ”   authorizing   the   Township,   or   a   third-party   approved   by   the   Township,   to   enter  into the residence due to the urgent conditions described by Mr. Huegel; and  WHEREAS ,   on   February   3,   2012,   Tom   Tracey,   the   Township   Construction   Official  issued   a   Notice   of   Unsafe   Structure   and   Notice   of   Imminent   Hazard   with   regard   to   the   property,  annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ C, ”   which   ordered   that   Mr.   Brumlop   fix   the   conditions   stated   or  demolish   the Property   by February 24, 2012 ; and  WHEREAS ,   on   March   13,   2012,   the   dangerous   conditions   at   the   Property   not   having  been   abated,   the   Township   filed   in   the   Superior   Court   of   New   Jersey   a   Verified   Complaint   and  Order to Show Cause seeking authorization to demolish the Property ; and  WHEREAS ,   on   April   19,   2012,   the   Honorable   Sebastian   P.   Lombardi,   Judge   of   the  Superior   Court,   entered   an   Order,   a   copy   of   which   is   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ D , ”  authorizing   the   Township   to   demolish   the   Property,   and   to   recoup   as   a   lien   its   costs   in  demolishing   the   Property,   in   bringing   the   Verified   Complaint   and   Order   to   Show   Cause,   and   in  performing any other work at the Property to ensure the public safety and welfare   (the   “ Order ” ) ;  WHEREAS ,   in   May   2012,   the   Township   received   bids   from   three   (3)   vendors   to  demolish the Property, which are annexed hereto as   Exhibit   “ E ” ; and  WHEREAS ,   T.   Fiore   Demolition,   Inc. ’ s   ( “ T.   Fiore ” )   bid   of   $15,000.00   to   demolish   the  Property was the lowest of those received; and  WHEREAS ,   on   September   11,   2012   T.   Fiore   demolished   the   property.   A   copy   of   date-  stamped photos of the demolition are annexed hereto as   Exhibits   “ F ” ; and

WHEREAS ,   the   Township   incurred   a   cost   of   $15,000.00   for   the   demolition   of   the  Property.   A   copy   of   the   Township ’ s   June   25,   2012   Purchase   Order   for   the   demolition   is  annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ G , ”   a   copy   of   T.   Fiore ’ s   September   14,   2012   invoice   to   the  Township   is   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ H , ”   and   a   copy   of   the   Township ’ s   September   28,   2012  check to T. Fiore for $15,000.00 is annexed hereto as   Exhibit   “ I ” ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   expended   $ 5,260.44   in   legal   fees   and   expenses   in  connection with this matter; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township's   Ordinances   permit   the   Township   to   place   a   lien   against   the  Property   in   the   amount   of   $ 20 , 260 . 44   for   expenses   and   fees   incurred   by   the   Township   in  handling this matter.  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE   that   a   lien   in   the   amount   of   $20,260.44   shall   be   placed  against   293   St.   Cloud   Avenue,   West   Orange   for   the   cost   of   services   required   to   demolish   the  structure and safeguard the Property .  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, MUNICIPAL CLERK   SUSAN McCARTNEY, COUNCIL PRESIDENT  Adopted: September 9, 2014  4841-0761-1421, v. 1

documents.title: RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING RETENTION OF T. SCRIVO, ESQ. FOR HAWKINS ELECTION OBJECTION

pages.text: 177-14  September 9, 2014  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   will   hold   a   general   municipal  election on November 4, 2014 (the   “ Election ” ); and  WHEREAS,   the   deadline   for   submission   of   petitions   of   nomination   (the   “ Petitions ” )   to   the  Municipal Clerk (the   “ Clerk ” ), pursuant to   N.J.S.A.   40:45-8, was September 2, 2014; and  WHEREAS,   Mayor   Robert   D.   Parisi   ( “ Mayor   Parisi ” ),   a   candidate   in   the   Election,   has  timely   submitted   Petitions   in   support   of   his   candidacy   to   the   Clerk,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40:45-8;  and  WHEREAS,   on   September   8,   2014,   the   Clerk   received   correspondence   from   Eldridge  Hawkins,   Esq.,   on   behalf   of   Mayoral   candidate   Eldridge   Hawkins,   Jr.,   objecting   to   the   Petitions  filed on behalf of Mayor Parisi (the   “ Hawkins Objection ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   the   Hawkins   Objection   alleges   that   Petitions   submitted   on   behalf   of   Mayor  Parisi   may be deficient for various reasons ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   Attorney   and   Assistant   Township   Attorney   have   recused  themselves   from   any   involvement   with   the   Clerk ’ s   consideration   and   review   of   the   Hawkins  Objection; and  WHEREAS,   it   is   necessary   for   the   Clerk   to   receive   the   benefit   of   independent   legal  advice   with   respect   to   her   consideration   and   review   of   the   Hawkins   Objection,   the   applicable  statutes, and any   potential legal challenge to the discharge of her duties as Clerk; and  WHEREAS,   the   Clerk   is   required,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   19:13-11,   to   make  determinations   with   regard   to   the   sufficiency   of   any   filed   Petition   by   no   later   than   September   11,  2014; and  WHEREAS,   in   light   of   the   emergent   and   time-sensitive   nature   of   the   Clerk ’ s   review   of  the   Hawkins   Objection,   and   in   light   of   the   general   importance   in   preserving   the   integrity   of   all  aspects   of   the   electoral   process   within   the   Township,   the   Law   Department   recommends   that   the

Township immediately retain independent counsel for the Clerk; and  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED ,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange,   that   Thomas   P.   Scrivo,   Esq.   of   McElroy,   Deutsch,   Mulvaney   &   Carpenter,   LLP ,   shall   be  retained   to   represent   the   Clerk   at   a   discounted   fixed   hourly   rate   of   $200   per   hour;   and   it   is  further  RESOLVED ,   that   this   Resolution   shall   be   published   and   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s  Office for reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J . Carnevale   Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   September 9, 2014  I hereby Certify funds are available from:   Account No.  JOHN GROSS, CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER  4819-7239-7598, v. 1

2417-14  AN ORDINANCE   REQUIRING THE INSTALLATION OF A  KEY   LOCK   BOX EMERGENCY ACCESS SYSTEM FOR USE BY THE  WEST ORANGE   FIRE DEPARTMENT DURING AN EMERGENCY OR ANY OTHER  ACTION DEEMED NECESSARY BY THE   WEST ORANGE   FIRE DEPARTMENT  AND ADDING   SECTION   4   TO CHAPTER   18   OF THE  MUNICIPAL CODE OF THE   TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  WHEREAS,   the   health,   safety,   and   welfare   of   the   citizens   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange   ( “ Township ” )   are   promoted   by   requiring   certain   structures   to   have   a   key   lock   box  installed   on   the   exterior   of   the   structure   to   aid   the   Township   F ire   Department   in   gaining   acces s   to  the structure when responding to calls for emergency service; and  WHEREAS ,   many buildings are equipped with automatic systems that may cause the  Township   Fire   Department   to   be   summoned   at   a   time   of   emergency   when   the   building   or  business   is   not   occupied   or   when   the   occupant   is   not   available   to   provide   the   Township   Fire  Department entry into the building; and  WHEREAS,   the   key   lock   box   emergency   access   system   will   eliminate   costly   damage  from   forcible   entry   into   buildings   equipped   with   automatic   systems   by   the   Township   Fire  Department checking for fire or other dangerous conditions; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   Fire   Department   can   reduce   the   potential   for   rapid   expansion  of fire and other hazardous conditions by quick access and decreas e   dangers for firefighters; and  WHEREAS,   the   key   lock   box   emergency   access   system   has   been   adopted   by   Section  506   of the   International Fire Code 2006, New Jersey Edition ;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   key   lock   box   emergency   access   system   will   operate   on   a   controlled  master key basis by the   Township   Fire Department; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   is   authorized   to   pass   this   Ordinanc e   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.  40:48-2, and pursuant to   the Township ’ s   police powers.  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED ,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township  of   West   Orange   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   that   there   should   be   created  a   Chapter   18,   Section   4   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township,   entitled   “ Key   Lock

2  Box Emergency Access System, ”   which shall provide as follows:  18 - 4   KEY   LOCK   BOX EMERGENCY ACCESS SYSTEM  18-4.1   Definitions  Automatic   Dialer   shall   refer   to   a   device   that   automatically   dials   and   relays   a   prerecorded  message to a central station or the fire department.  Automatic   Fire   Suppression   System   refers   to   a   system   or   assembly   of   piping,   valves,   controls,  and   sprinklers,   which   are   designed   and   installed   to   comply   with   the   National   Fire   Protection  Association   (NFPA)   standards,   which   utilize   water,   foam,   carbon   dioxide,   or   other   gas   to  automatically react to suppress fire.  Fire Official   shall refer to the Fire Chief or his /her   designee.  Central   Station   shall   refer   to   an   office   of   a   private   company   to   which   remote   alarm   and  supervisory   signaling   devices   are   transmitted   and   where   personnel   are   in   attendance   at   all  times to supervise the circuits and investigate signals.  Key   Lock   Box   shall   refer   to   a   secure   rapid   entry   system   that   is   designed   to   be   used   by   Fire  Department   Personnel   in   the   event   of   an   emergency   to   gain   entry   into   a   struct ure   by   using   the  enclosed   owner- provided   key(s).   This   box   is   usually   mounted   on   the   exterior   of   the   building  in   a   location   that   is   specified   by   the   Fire   Official   and   Construction   Official .   All   boxes   shall  be   UL   (Underwriters   Laboratories)   certified ,   Knox   Box   brand,   and   approved   by   the   Fire  Official   and Construction Official .  Standpipe   System   shall   refer   to   a   wet   or   dry   system   of   piping,   valves,   outlets   and   related  equipment   designed   to   provide   water   at   specified   pressures   and   installed   exclusively   for   the  fighting of fires.  18- 4 . 2   Structures to Be Equipped with   a Key Lock Box  Unless   otherwise   determined   in   writing   by   the   Fire   Official,   t he   following   structures   shall   be  equipped   with   a   Key   Lock   Box   of   a   type   and   size   and   at   a   location   approved   by   the   Fire  Official   and Construction Official :  (a)   All   new   commercial   buildings   shall   be   equipped   with   a   Key   Lock   Box   prior   to   the  issuance of the   certificate   of occupancy.  (c)   All   existing   commercial   buil dings   constructing   additions,   major   renovations,   or  changes   of   use   tha t   require   Township   Zoning   or   Planning   Board   approval ,   shall  be equipped with   a   Key Lock Box   prior to the   issuance of any necessary   permit s .  (d)   All   existing   commercial   buildings   with   new   occupancies   by   tenants   shall   be  equipped   with   a   Key   Lock   Box   prior   to   the   issuance   of   a   Certificate   of   Continued  Occupancy.

3  (e)   Each   building   protected   by   an   automatic   fire   suppression   and/or   standpipe   system  and not manned 24 hour s a day, 7   day s a week .  (f)   Each   building   protected   by   an   automatic   fire   alarm   system   (automatic   dialer,  central   station,   external   audible/visual   alarm)   and   not   manned   24   hour s   a   day,   7  day s a week.  (g)   Multi-family   residential   structures   that   have   restricted   access   through   locked  doors   but   have   a   common   corridor   for   access   to   the   living   units ,   including   but   not  limited to condominium buildings.  (h)   Schools, whether public or private.  (i)   Governmental   structures   and   nursing   care   facilities ,   unless   the   building   is   staffed  or open   24 hour s a day, 7   day s a week.  18-4. 3   Key Lock Box Contents  The   property   owner   is   required   at   all   times   to   keep   a   key(s)   in   the   Key   Lock   Box   that   will  allow   access   to   the   structure.   The   Key   Lock   Box   shall   contain,   but   not   be   limited   to,   the  following items   as designated by the Fire Official:  (a)   Labeled   keys   to   locked   points   of   ingress   and   egress,   whether   in   interior   or   exterior   of  such   buildings;  (b)   Labeled keys to the locked mechanical rooms;  (c)   Labeled keys to locked elevator rooms and controls;  (d)   Labeled keys to any fence or secured areas;  (e)   Labeled   keys   to   areas   of   the   building   where   fire   alarm   panels   and   fire   protection  systems   are located;  (f)   Labeled keys to any other areas that may be required by the Fire Official;  (g)   A   card   containing   the   emergency   contact   names   and   p hone   numbers   for   such  buildings;  (h)   Floor plans of the rooms within the building showing   locations of shut- offs;  (i)   Hazardous materials information; and  (j)   An inventory of the keys.  The   property   owner   shall   notify   the   Township   Fire   Department   when   any   or   all   of   the   locks  or   keys   have   been   changed   and   shall   keep   the   immediate   area   of   the   Key   Lock   B ox   free   and  clear of any and all obstructions.

4  �-.4   Registration and   Installation of Key Lock Boxes  The   property   owner   shall   apply   for   a   registration   for   a   Key   Lock   B ox   on   forms   provided   by  and   obtained   from   the   Fire   Official .   A   registration   is   required   prior   to   the   installation   of   a  Key   Lock   B ox   in   order   to   verify   the   proper   size,   mounting   location   and   installation   of   said  Key   Lock   B ox.   The   Key   Lock   B ox   shall   be   install ed   at   a   location   approved   by   the   Fire  Official   and   the   Construction   Official .   No   Key   Lock   B ox   shall   be   installed,   voluntarily   or  otherwise,   without   first   obtaining   the   approval   of   the   Fire   Official   and   the   Construction  Official .   The   property   owner   shall   be   responsible   for   the   cost   to   purchase,   install,   and  maintain the   Key Lock B ox.  18-4. 5   Compliance  All   existing   buildings   required   to   have   a   Key   Lock   Box   shall   comply   with   this   Ordinance   one  (1)   yea r   from   its   effective   date.   All   newly   constructed   buildings   not   yet   occupied   or   buildings  currently   under   construction   and   all   buildings   or   businesses   applying   for   a   certificate   of  occupancy   shall   comply   immediately   upon   passage   of   this   Ordinance.   In   any   event,   the  owner   of   any   structure   in   the   Township   shall   comply   with   this   ordinance   within   sixty   (60)  days   from   receipt   of   written   notice   from   the   Fire   Official.   The   cost   of   purchasing   and  installing,   along   with   any   cost   associated   with   implementation   of   the   program   at   a   specific  property, will be   borne   by the   property owner .  18-4. 6   Penalties  Any   property   owner   failing   to   comply   with   this   Ordinance   shall   be   subject   to   a   fine   of   not  more than Three Hundred ($300) Dollars for each day of violation or non-compliance.  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor   Susan McCartney ,   Council President  K aren J. Carnevale ,   Municipal Clerk  Introduced: August 12, 2014  Adop t ed: September 9, 2014  2417-14

5  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  Authority   for   this   proposed   legislation   is   derived   from   N.J.S.A.   40:48-2,   entitling  municipalities   to   make   and   enforce   ordinances   and   regulations   it   deems   necessary   and   proper   for  the   protection   of   persons   and   property,   and   for   the   preservation   of   the   public   health,   safety   and  welfare   of   the   municipality   and   its   inhabitants.   T he   key   lock   box   emergency   access   system   has  also   been adopted by Section   506   of the   International Fire Co de 2006, New Jersey Edition.  The   purpose   of   the   proposed   legislation   is   to   promote   the   health,   safety,   and   welfare   of  the   Township ’ s   citizens   and   business   owners   by   allowing   the   Township   Fire   Department   to   gain  access   to   structures   when   same   is   not   occupied   or   when   the   occupants   are   unable   to   grant   ingress  to   the   Fire   Department.   The   improved   access   will   reduce   the   need   for   forced   entry   into  structures   and   should   avoid   costly   and   time-consuming   efforts   in   gaining   access   to   locked  structures during an emergency.  By   enabling   quick   access   to   buildings,   the   proposed   legislation   will   also   reduce   the   risk  of   rapid   extension   of   fire   and   other   hazardous   conditions .   Such   a   reduction   will   decrease   dangers  to firefighters ,   and   help minimize the   spread of property damage.  4837-2482-5626, v. 1

2418-14  AN ORDINANCE   REQUIRING THE INSTALLATION OF A   FIRE DEPARTMENT  CONNECTION INDICATOR LIGHT   FOR USE BY THE   WEST ORANGE   FIRE  DEPARTMENT AND ADDING   SECTION   5   TO CHAPTER   18   OF THE MUNICIPAL  CODE OF THE   TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  WHEREAS,   the   health,   safety,   and   welfare   of   the   citizens   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange   ( “ Township ” )   are   promoted   by   requiring   certain   structures   to   instal l   a   connection  indicator   light   on   the   exterior   of   the   structure   to   aid   the   Township   Fire   Department   in   locating  Fire Department Connections ( “ FDCs ” ) in emergency situations ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   Fire   Department   can   reduce   the   potential   for   rapid   extension  of   fire   and   other   hazardous   conditions   and   decreas e   dangers   for   firefighters   by   quick   access   to  FDCs ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   Construction   Official   and   Fire   Official   have   reviewed   this  ordinance   and   recommend its passage; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   is   authorized   to   pass   this   Ordinanc e   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.  40:48-2, and pursuant to   the Township ’ s   po lice powers .  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED ,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township  of   West   Orange   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   that   there   should   be   created  a   Chapter   18,   Section   5   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township,   entitled   “ Fire  Department Connection Indicator Light , ”   which shall provide as follows:  �-5   FIRE DEPARTMENT CONNECTION INDICATOR LIGHT  �-.1   The   owner   of   a   new   s tructure ,   an   ex isting   structure   undergoing   construction  requiring   a   new   or   revised   Certificate   of   Occupancy ,   or   an   existing   structure  undergoing   a   change   of   use,   having   a   sprinkler   and/or   standpipe   connection   either  attached   to   or   within   five   feet   of   said   structure ,   shall   be   required   to   install,   maintain  and   illuminate   a   weatherproof   light   fixture   containing   a   100   watt   light   source,   red   in  color,   mounted   on   the   structure   in   the   immediate   vicinity   of   the   sprinkler   or  standpipe   connection   at   a   height   of   seven   feet   above   the   connection.   The   exact   final

2  placement   and   design   of   the   light   fixture   shall   be   set   forth   on   plans   submitted   to   and  approved by the Fire   Chief   or his/ her designee   and the Construction Official .  �-.2   The   owner   of   a   new   s tructure,   an   existing   structure   undergoing   construction  requiring   a   new   or   revised   Certificate   of   Occupancy ,   or   an   existing   structure  undergoing   a   change   of   use,   having   a   sprinkler   and/or   standpipe   connection   remotely  located   at   a   distance   in   excess   of   five   feet   of   said   structure   shall   cause   to   be   placed,  installed,   maintained   and   illuminated   a   freestanding,   weatherproof   light   fixture  containing   a   100   watt   light   source,   red   in   color,   mounted   immediately   behind   the  fixture   at   a   height   of   four   feet.   The   exact   final   placement   and   design   of   the   light  fixture   shall   be   set   forth   on   plans   submitted   to   and   approved   by   the   Fire   Chief   or  his/ her designee   and the Construction Official .  �-.3   In   certain   cases,   based   upon   extraordinary   condition s ,   the   Fire   C hief,   or   his/ her  designee,   shall   have   the   right   to   grant   a   waiver   of   the   requirements   imposed   by   this  section,   on   an   individual   basis;   however,   some   form   of   other   substitute   identification  will be required.  �-.4   The   owner   of   any   structure   in   the   Township   which   falls   within   the   requirements   of  this   action   shall   cause   the   structure   to   be   brought   into   compliance   with   this   Section  within   one   (1)   year   from   its   effective   date,   or   60   days   from   receipt   of   written   notice  from the Fire   Chief, or his / her designee , whichever is sooner.  �-.5   Any   property   owner   failing   to   comply   with   this   Ordinance   shall   be   subject   to   a   fine  of   not   more   than   Three   Hundred   ($300)   Dollars   for   each   day   of   violation   or   non-  compliance.  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor   Susan McCartney, Council President  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk  Introduced: August 12, 2014  Adop t ed: September 9, 2014

3  2418-14  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  Authority   for   this   proposed   legislation   is   derived   from   N.J.S.A.   40:48-2,   entitling  municipalities   to   make   and   enforce   ordinances   and   regulations   it   deems   necessary   and   proper   for  the   protection   of   persons   and   property,   and   for   the   preservation   of   the   public   health,   safety   and  welfare of the municipality and its inhabitants.  The   purpose   of   the   proposed   legislation   is   to   promote   the   health,   safety,   and   welfare   of  the   Township ’ s   citizens   and   business   owners   by   allowing   the   Township   Fire   Department   to  locate   and   gain   access   to   Fire   Department   Connections   ( “ FDCs ” )   as   quickly   as   possible   during  emergency   situations.   FDC s   are   intended   to   provide   sprinkler   systems   with   a   means   to   receive  water   support   from   a   Fire   D epartment   pumping   apparatus.   This   ability   is   necessary   when  sprinkler   systems   need   supplemental   water   support,   and   when   e vents   such   as   floods,   hurricanes,  and   similar   events   interrupt   utility-provided   water   because   of   loss   of   power   to   pumping   stations  or   damage   to   the   water   supply   infrastructure   piping.   If   these   events   were   to   interrupt   the  automatic   water   supply   to   a   sprinkler   system,   then   the   only   possibility   of   providing   water   to   the  sprinkler   would   be   through   the   FDC.   By   enabling   quick   access   to   FDCs ,   the   proposed  legislation   will   reduce   the   risk   of   rapid   extension   of   fire   and   other   hazardous   conditions .   Such   a  reduction   will decrease dangers to firefighters ,   and   help minimize the   spread of property damage.  4833-4677-0202, v. 1

2419-14  AN ORDINANCE   AMENDING   CHAPTER   4, SECTION 35.2   OF THE REVISED  GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  I.   PURPOSE  WHEREAS ,   the   existence   of   stray,   feral   and   otherwise   unconfined   wildlife   in   the  Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   creates   a   hazard   to   the   health   of   Township  residents and their pets; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township ’ s   Health   Department   (the   “ Department ” )   reports   that   during  the   period   between   2010   and   2013,   approximately   four-hundred   and   thirty   (430)   stray   cat  complaints   were   registered   with   the   Department ’ s   Animal   Control   Division   ( “ ACD ” ),   and   five-  hundred and seventeen (517) stray cats were impounded ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Department   reports   that   during   the   period   between   January   1,   2014   and  June   30,   2014,   sixty-eight   (68)   stray   cat   complaints   were   registered   with   the   ACD,   and   eighty-  one (81) stray cats were impounded; and  WHEREAS,   the   intentional   feeding   of   stray,   feral   and   otherwise   unconfined   wildlife  within   the   Township   is   a   major   cause   of   the   proliferation   of   stray,   feral   and   otherwise   unconfined  wildlife in the Township;   and  WHEREAS,   Chapter   4,   Section   35.2   of   the   Township ’ s   Revised   General   Ordinances  ( “ Section   35.2 ” )   prohibits   the   feeding   of   wildlife,   excluding   confined   wildlife,   in   any   public   park   or  Township-owned property; and  WHEREAS,   the   prohibition   set   forth   in   Section   35.2,   insofar   as   it   is   limited   in   application  to   public   parks   and   Township-owned   property,   has   proven   insufficient   to   prevent   the  aforementioned hazard to the health of Township residents and their pets;

2  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New   Jersey,   that  Chapter   4 ,   Section   35.2   the   Revised   General   Ordinance s   of   the   Township   shall   be   amended   as  follows:  II.   CHAPTER   4,   SECTION   35.2   SHALL   BE   AND   HEREBY   IS   AMENDED   AND  SUPERSEDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS:  4-35.2   Prohibited Conduct  No   person   shall   feed   any   wildlife,   excluding   confined   wildlife,   anywhere   within   the  Township.  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED,   that   if   any   section,   paragraph,   subsection,   clause   or  provision   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   adjudged   by   the   courts   to   be   invalid,   such   adjudication   shall  apply   only   to   the   section,   paragraph,   subsection,   clause   or   provision   so   adjudicated,   and   the  remainder of the Ordinance shall be deemed valid and effective.  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED ,   that   any   ordinances   or   parts   thereof   in   conflict   with   the  provisions of this Ordinance are repealed to the extent of such conflict.  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED ,   that   this   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   passage   and  publication in accordance with law.  Susan McCartney   Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  Council President  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk  Introduction :   August 12, 2014  Adopted: September 9, 2014  4826-2165-6348, v. 1

2420-14  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING  CHAPTER 7, TRAFFIC, SUBSECT   I ON 7-23.1, TURN PROHIBITIONS,  OF THE REVISED GENERAL ORDINANCES OF  THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  BE IT ORDAINED,   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, in the  County of Essex, State of New Jersey as follows:  Section 1.   Subsection 7-23.1, Turn Prohibitions, of the Revised General Ordinances  of the Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows: ( italic type  denotes deletion,   bold type denotes addition):  Intersection   Turn   Hours/   Movement Prohibited  Prohibited   Days  Oak Ridge Road and   Right   7:30 a.m.   N orthbound on Oak Ridge  Hazel Avenue   to   Road   to eastbound on Hazel  9:00 a.m.   ;   Avenue  2:30 p.m.  to  4:00 p.m.  School Days  Oak Ridge Road and   Left   7:30 a.m.   S outhbound on Oak Ridge  Hazel Avenue   to   Road to eastbound on Hazel  9:00 a.m.   ;   Avenue  2:30 p.m.  to  4:00 p.m.  School Days  Birch Street and   Right   7:30 a.m.   N orthbound on Birch Street  Hazel Avenue   to   to eastbound on Hazel  Avenue  9:00 a.m.   ;  2:30 p.m.  to  4:00 p.m.  School Days  Hazel Avenue and   Left   7:30 a.m.   S outhbound on Hazel  Avenue  Hazel Avenue   to   School easterly driveway to  School easterly   9:00 a.m.   ;   eastbound on Hazel  Avenue  driveway   2:30 p.m.  to  4:00 p.m.  School Days

Section 2.   If any provisions of this Ordinance or application thereof , under any  circumstances, is held invalid, the invalidity shall not affect any of the provisions or applications  of this Ordinance that can be given effect without the valid provision(s) or application and to this  end, the provisions of this Ordinance are severable.  Section 3.   All other Ordinances or parts of Ordinances inconsistent with this  Ordinance are hereby repealed.  Section 4.   This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication   in  accordance with law and upon approval of the New Jersey Department of Transportation if  required by   S tatute.  Robert D. 2420-14  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance ,   to improve both vehicular and pedestrian safety in the vicinity of the  Hazel Avenue School ,   will prohibit certain turning movements on Hazel Avenue from  intersecting streets and a school driveway on school days during the hours of 7:30 A.M. to 9:00  A.M. and 2:30 P.M. to 4:00 P.M.   T hese proposed regulations will prohibit turning movements in  an easterly direction on Hazel Avenue from Oak Ridge   R oad, Birch Street and the easterly  school driveway. This will minimize conflicts between vehicles traveling   i n opposite directions  on Hazel Avenue and keep most traffic flowing westerly on Hazel Avenue with the school  located to the right on the northerly side of the street.

2422-14  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  To   provide   additional   on   street   parking   in   the   Valley   section   of   West  Orange,   this   Ordinance   will   rescind   the   on   street   parking   prohibition   on   the  easterly   side   of   Rollinson   Street   between   Pillot   Place   and   Swaine   Place.   Parking  will   still   be   prohibited   at   all   times   on   that   side   between   Mitchell   Street   and   Pillot  Place   and   the   one   block   from   Swaine   Place   to   Northfield   Avenue.   However ,   to  allow   for   street   cleaning   and   maintenance ,   parking   on   the   east erly   side   of   Rollinson  Street   between   Pillot   Place   and   Swaine   Place   will   be   prohibited   on   Mondays  between   the   hours   of   8:00   A.M.   and   10:00   A.M.   Finally   overnight   parking   is  allowed   on   Rollinson   Street   where   parking   is   allowed   and   this   Ordinance   addresses  that   by   amending   Revised   General   Ordinance   7-14.1c   which   lists   certain  exceptions to the overnight prohibition.

LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This   legislation   is   intended   to   update   the   Township ’ s   Building   Subcode   Fees   within   its  Construction   Permit   Fee   Ordinance   to   correct   a   long-standing   typographical   error.   On   May   23,  2000,   Ordinance   No.   1703-00   was   adopted,   which   provided   that   the   Building   Subcode   Fee   for  new   construction   would   be   $0.018   per   cubic   foot   of   building   or   structure   volume.   On   April   26,  2005,   Ordinance   No.   2012-05   was   adopted,   which   increased   the   Building   Subcode   Fee   for   new  construction   to   $0.025   per   cubic   foot   of   building   or   structure   volume.   On   October   2,   2007,  Ordinance   No.   2146-07   was   adopted,   which   add ed   a   flat   fee   to   be   charged   for   retaining   walls,  shed   type   structures   and   swimming   pools,   and   declare d   that   the   maximum   fee   that   can   be  charged   for   obtaining   a   temporary   certificate   of   occupancy   is   $30.00,   in   accordance   with   State  regulations.   However,   in   Ordinance   No.   2146-07 ,   a   decimal   point   was   misplaced,   mistakenly  changing   the   Building   Subcode   Fee   for   new   construction   to   $0.25   per   cubic   foot   of   building   or  structure   volume,   increasing   that   fee   by   100   times.   T he   Construction   Official   has   stated   that   the  actual   fee   charged   has   been   $0.025   per   cubic   foot   of   building   or   structure   volume   since   he  started   working   for   the   Township   in   2006 .   It   has   recently   come   to   the   Construction   Official ’ s  attention   that   the   fee   listed   in   the   Ordinance   is   incorrect.   Thus,   the   proposed   ordinance   would  correct the typographical error.  4839-0767-7724, v. 1

2424-14  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING CHAPTER   25 , SECTION   9 .1 2   OF THE  REVISED GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  ( HOURS OF OPERATION FOR BUSINESSES OPERATING IN  RESIDENTIAL ZONES IN THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE )  BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   MUNICIPAL   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF  WEST   ORANGE,   NEW   JERSEY   that   Chapter   25 - 9 .1 2   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of  the Township of West Orange be and   are   hereby amended as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  The purpose of this ordinance is to   set limitations on the hours of operation for any  business operating   within 200 feet of any   residential zone.  II.   CHAPTER   25,   SECTION   9. 1  2   SHALL   BE   AND   HEREBY   IS   AMENDED   AND  SUPERCEDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS:  �-.12   Hours of Operation of Businesses Operating within Residential Districts  a.   For purposes of this section,   Business   shall mean   a   restaurant, retail food  establishments,   retail sales   and personal services business .   Businesses   shall not  include   an establishment that sells   prescription or non-prescription  p harmaceuticals, or   an   establishment provid es   emergency   medical   care services  provided that the establishment do es   not perform any other commercial functions  during the hours prohibited under subsection (e) of this ordinance.  b.   No business es   established   within 200 feet of   any residential zone shall have hours  of operation with opening of the business earlier than   6:00   a.m. and closing of the  business later than   12:00   midnight.  c.   No deliveries to such businesses shall likewise commence prior to   6:00 a.m.   and  all deliveries must be completed before   12:00   midnight   except as may be  approved by any reviewing board.  d.   Nothing herein shall preclude the reviewing board hearing any application for  establishing more restrictive hours for operations or delivery, based upon the  nature of the proposed business operation and other facts and circumstances  considered by the board.  e.   Hours of operation or delivery beyond that authorized by this Ordinance shall be  prohibited.  f.   The foregoing restrictions shall not apply to any business already established and  in operation on the date this ordinance is adopted where hours of operation differ

from the requirements of this subsection. No business already established and in  operation as of the date of this subsection shall increase or expand hours of  operation beyond existing hours of operation or the hours permitted by this  subsection, whichever is the greater.  g.   Any businesses may obtain a permit to operate during the hours prohibited under  subsection (e) of this ordinance on a temporary basis not to exceed one month  after applying to and receiving approval from the Township of West Orange  Planning Board or the Township of West Orange Zoning Board of Adjustment.  h.   Nothing within this ordinance shall alter or affect the requirements set forth in  Chapter 4, Section 9 of the Municipal Code of the Township of West Orange and  any Business operating during all or part of the hours of 12:00 midnight and 6:00  a.m. shall be required to comply with the requirements of Chapter 4, Section 9 of  the Municipal Code of the Township of West Orange.  I I I .   REPEAL OF CONFLICTING ORDINANCES  Any   Ordinances   of   the   Township   which   are   in   conflict   with   this   Ordinance   are   hereby  repealed to the extent of such conflict.  I V .   SEVERABILITY  If   any   part   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   deemed   invalid,   such   parts   shall   be   severed   and   th e  invalidity thereof shall not a ffect the remaining parts of this Ordinance.  V.   EFFECTIVE DATE  This   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   final   passage   and   publication   in   accordance   with  the law.  Robert D. Parisi,   Mayor  Susan McCartney,   Council President   Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk  Introduced: September 9, 2014  Adopted: September 23, 2014

2424-14  Legislative History  This   ordinance   is   drafted   to   limit   the   hours   of   operations   of   businesses   located   in   residential  districts   within   the   Township   of   West   Orange   or   located   within   two   hundred   ( 200  )   feet   of   a   residential  district .   The   limits   as   to   the   hours   of   operation   of   businesses   are   limited   only   to   businesses   located   in  residential   districts   or   within   two   hundred   ( 200  )   feet   of   a   residential   district   in   compliance   with   Fasino   v.  Mayor   and   Member   of   the   Borough   Council   of   the   Borough   of   Montvale ,   122   N.J.   Super.   304   (Law   Div.  1973)   which   deemed   unconstitutional   a   general   closing   ordinance   passed   by   the   Borough   of   Montvale  that   governed   hours   of   operations   for   all   businesses   within   a   municipality.   In   addition,   this   ordinance   is  drafted   consistent   with   the   Appellate   Division ’ s   holding   in   Fulbrook   v.   City   of   Camden ,   2013   WL  5942128   (N.J.   App.   Div.   2013)   wherein   the   Appellate   Division   affirmed   a   trial   court ’ s   finding   that   an  ordinance   limiting   the   hours   of   operations   for   certain   businesses   within   Residential   Zones   or   within   two  hundred   (200)   feet   of   a   Residential   Zone   represented   a   valid   exercise   of   the   City   of   Camden ’ s   police  power.  Similarly,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   seeks   to   regulate   the   hours   of   operations   for   business  located   within   two   hundred   (200)   feet   of   a   residential   district   as   an   exercise   of   its   police   powers   and   in  support   of   the   general   welfare   of   the   residents   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange.   Modeled   after   the  ordinance   addressed   in   Fulbrook   v.   City   of   Camden ,   this   ordinance   seeks   to   maintain   the   esthetic   and  serenity   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange ’ s   residential   districts   for   the   benefit   of   the   residents   who   reside  in   those   districts   in   an   effort   to   minimize   the   amount   of   debris,   noise,   and   activity   that   often  accompanies   commercial   business   which   is   particularly   detrimental   to   a   residential   district   where  commercial businesses are fewer.  4827-8281-2957, v. 1

#2100080 v1  100181-33129  ORDINANCE   2425 -14  BOND   ORDINANCE   AUTHORIZING   VARIOUS  IMPROVEMENTS   IN   CONNECTION   WITH   THE  CAPITAL   ASSESSMENT   IN   RESPECT   OF   THE   JOINT  MEETING   OF   ESSEX   AND   UNION   COUNTIES   IN   AND  FOR   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   IN   THE  COUNTY   OF   ESSEX,   NEW   JERSEY,   APPROPRIATING  $  1,300,000   THEREFOR   AND   AUTHORIZING   THE  ISSUANCE   OF   $ 1,300,000   BONDS   OR   NOTES   TO  FINANCE PART OF THE COST THEREOF.  BE   IT   ORDAINED,   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST  ORANGE,   IN   THE   COUNTY   OF   ESSEX,   NEW   JERSEY   (not   less   than   two-thirds   of   all  members thereof affirmatively concurring) AS FOLLOWS:  SECTION 1:  The   improvements   described   in   Section   3   of   this   bond   ordinance   (the   “ Improvements ” )  are   hereby   authorized   to   be   undertaken   by   the   Township   of   West   Orange,   New   Jersey   (the  “ Township ” )   as   general   improvements.   For   the   said   Improvements   there   is   hereby   appropriated  the   amount   of   $ 1,300,000  .   No   down   payment   is   required   by   the   Local   Bond   Law   of   the   State   of  New   Jersey,   constituting   Chapter   2   of   Title   40A   of   the   New   Jersey   Statutes,   as   amended   and  supplemented   (the   “ Local   Bond   Law ” )   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:2-11(c)   as   the   bond   ordinance  authorizes   obligations   solely   for   a   purpose   described   in   N.J.S.A.   40A:2-7(d),   as   more   fully  explained in Section 6(e) of this ordinance.  SECTION 2:  In   order   to   finance   the   additional   cost   of   the   Improvements,   negotiable   bonds   of   the  Township   are   hereby   authorized   to   be   issued   in   the   principal   amount   of   $ 1,300,000   (the  “ Bonds ” )   pursuant   to   the   provisions   of   the   Local   Bond   Law.   In   anticipation   of   the   issuance   of  the   Bonds   and   to   temporarily   finance   said   improvements   or   purposes,   negotiable   bond  anticipation   notes   of   the   Township   are   hereby   authorized   to   be   issued   in   the   principal   amount   not  exceeding   $ 1,300,000   pursuant   to   the   provisions   of   the   Local   Bond   Law   (the   “ Bond   Anticipation  Notes ”   or   “ Notes ” ).  SECTION 3:  (a)   The   Improvements   authorized   and   the   purposes   for   which   obligations   are   to   be   issued  is   to   pay   the   Township ’ s   capital   assessment   in   respect   of   the   Joint   Meeting   of   Essex   and   Union  Counties   (the   “ Joint   Meeting ” ),   which   will   be   used   by   the   Joint   Meeting   to   finance   upgrades   to  the   Joint   Meeting ’ s   wastewater   treatment   plant   improvements ,   including ,   (i)   improvements   to  aeration   equipment ,   (ii)   improvements   to   digester   system   and   (i i i)   improvements   to   dewatering  facility ,   and   including   all   structures   and   appurtenances,   work   or   materials   necessary   therefor   or

#2100080 v1  100181-33129  incidental thereto .  (b)   The   estimated   maximum   amount   of   Bonds   or   Notes   to   be   issued   for   the   purpose   of  financing a portion of the cost of the Improvements is $ 1,300,000 .  (c)   The   estimated   cost   of   the   Improvements   is   $ 1,300,000   which   amount   represents   the  initial appropriation made by the Township.  SECTION 4.  All   Bond   Anticipation   Notes   issued   hereunder   shall   mature   at   such   times   as   may   be  determined   by   the   chief   financial   officer   of   the   Township   (the   “ Chief   Financial   Officer ” );  provided   that   no   Note   shall   mature   later   than   one   year   from   its   date.   The   Notes   shall   bear  interest   at   such   rate   or   rates   and   be   in   such   form   as   may   be   determined   by   the   Chief   Financial  Officer.   The   Chief   Financial   Officer   shall   determine   all   matters   in   connection   with   Notes   issued  pursuant   to   this   ordinance,   and   the   signature   of   the   Chief   Financial   Officer   upon   the   Notes   shall  be   conclusive   evidence   as   to   all   such   determinations.   All   Notes   issued   hereunder   may   be  renewed   from   time   to   time   subject   to   the   provisions   of   Section   8(a)   of   the   Local   Bond   Law.   The  Chief   Financial   Officer   is   hereby   authorized   to   sell   part   or   all   of   the   Notes   from   time   to   time   at  public   or   private   sale   and   to   deliver   them   to   the   purchasers   thereof   upon   receipt   of   payment   of  the   purchase   price   plus   accrued   interest   from   their   dates   to   the   date   of   delivery   thereof.   The  Chief   Financial   Officer   is   directed   to   report   in   writing   to   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township  at   the   meeting   next   succeeding   the   date   when   any   sale   or   delivery   of   the   Notes   pursuant   to   this  ordinance   is   made.   Such   report   must   include   the   amount,   the   description,   the   interest   rate   and  the maturity schedule of the Notes sold, the price obtained and the name of the purchaser.  SECTION 5:  The   capital   budget   of   the   Township   is   hereby   amended   to   conform   with   the   provisions   of  this   ordinance   to   the   extent   of   any   inconsistency   herewith.   The   resolution   in   the   form  promulgated   by   the   Local   Finance   Board   showing   full   detail   of   the   amended   capital   budget   and  capital   program   as   approved   by   the   Director,   Division   of   Local   Government   Services,  Department   of   Community   Affairs,   State   of   New   Jersey   is   on   file   with   the   Township   Clerk   and   is  available for public inspection.  SECTION 6:  The following additional matters are hereby determined, declared, recited and stated:  (a)   The   Improvements   described   in   Section   3   of   this   bond   ordinance   are   not   current  expenses,   and   are   capital   improvements   or   properties   that   the   Township   may   lawfully   make   or  acquire   as   general   improvements,   and   no   part   of   the   cost   thereof   has   been   or   shall   be   specially  assessed on property specially benefited thereby.  (b)   The   average   period   of   usefulness   of   the   Improvements,   within   the   limitations   of   the  Local   Bond   Law,   taking   into   consideration   the   respective   amounts   of   all   obligations   authorized

#2100080 v1  100181-33129  for   the   several   purposes,   according   to   the   reasonable   life   thereof   computed   from   the   date   of   the  Bonds authorized by this bond ordinance, is   twenty   ( 2 0)   years.  (c)   The   Supplemental   Debt   Statement   required   by   the   Local   Bond   Law   has   been   duly  prepared   and   filed   in   the   office   of   the   Township   Clerk   and   a   complete   executed   duplicate   thereof  has   been   filed   in   the   office   of   the   Director,   Division   of   Local   Government   Services,   Department  of   Community   Affairs,   State   of   New   Jersey.   Such   statement   shows   that   the   gross   debt   of   the  Township,   as   defined   in   the   Local   Bond   Law,   is   increased   by   the   authorization   of   the   Bonds   and  Notes   provided   in   this   bond   ordinance   by   $ 1,300,000   and   the   obligations   authorized   herein   will  be within all debt limitations prescribed by the Local Bond Law.  (d)   An   aggregate   amount   not   exceeding   $ 4  00,000   for   items   of   expense   listed   in   and  permitted   under   Section   20   of   the   Local   Bond   Law   is   included   in   the   estimated   cost   of   the  Improvements, as indicated herein.  (e)   This   bond   ordinance   authorized   obligations   of   the   Township   solely   for   a   purpose  described   in   N.J.S.A.   40A:2-7(d).   This   purpose   is   in   the   public   interest   and   is   for   the   health,  welfare,   convenience   or   betterment   of   the   inhabitants   of   the   Township.   The   amounts   to   be  expended   for   this   purpose   pursuant   to   this   bond   ordinance   are   not   unreasonable   or   exorbitant,  and   the   issuance   of   the   obligations   authorized   by   this   bond   ordinance   will   not   materially   impair  the   credit   of   the   Township   or   substantially   reduce   its   ability   to   pay   punctually   the   principal   of  and   the   interest   on   its   debts   and   to   supply   other   essential   public   improvements   and   services.   The  Local   Finance   Board   in   the   Division   of   Local   Government   Services   of   the   Department   of  Community   Affairs   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey   has   heretofore   made   a   determination   to   this   effect  and   has   caused   its   consent   to   be   endorsed   upon   a   certified   copy   of   this   bond   ordinance   as   passed  upon first reading.  SECTION 7:  Any   funds   received   from   time   to   time   by   the   Township   as   contributions   in   aid   of  financing   the   purposes   described   in   Section   3   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   used   for   financing   said  Improvements   by   application   thereof   either   to   direct   payment   of   the   cost   of   said   Improvements  or   to   the   payment   or   reduction   of   the   authorization   of   the   obligations   of   the   Township   authorized  therefor   by   this   Bond   Ordinance.   Any   such   funds   received   may,   and   all   such   funds   so   received  which   are   not   required   for   direct   payment   of   the   cost   of   said   Improvements   shall,   be   held   and  applied   by   the   Township   as   funds   applicable   only   to   the   payment   of   obligations   of   the   Township  authorized by this Bond Ordinance.  SECTION 8:  The   full   faith   and   credit   of   the   Township   are   hereby   pledged   to   the   punctual   payment   of  the   principal   of   and   interest   on   the   obligations   authorized   by   this   bond   ordinance.   The  obligations   shall   be   direct,   unlimited   obligations   of   the   Township,   and   the   Township   shall   be  obligated   to   levy   ad   valorem   taxes   upon   all   the   taxable   property   within   the   Township   for   the  payment of the obligations and the interest thereon without limitation of rate or amount.

#2100080 v1  100181-33129  SECTION 9:  This   Bond   Ordinance   constitutes   a   declaration   of   official   intent   under   Treasury  Regulation   Section   1.150-2.   The   Township   reasonably   expects   to   pay   expenditures   with   respect  to   the   Improvements   prior   to   the   date   that   Township   incurs   debt   obligations   under   this   Bond  Ordinance.   The   Township   reasonably   expects   to   reimburse   such   expenditures   with   the   proceeds  of   debt   to   be   incurred   by   the   Township   under   this   Bond   Ordinance.   The   maximum   principal  amount   of   debt   expected   to   be   issued   for   payment   of   the   costs   of   the   Improvements   is  $ 1,300,000 .  SECTION 10:  This   bond   ordinance   shall   take   effect   20   days   after   the   first   publication   thereof   after   final  adoption, as provided by the Local Bond Law. 2-17 DEPARTMENT OF RECREATION AND CULTURAL AFFAIRS.  2-17.1   Department Generally.  a.   There   shall   be   a   Department   of   Recreation   and   Cultural   Affairs,   the   head   of   which   shall   be  the   Director.   Prior   to   his/her   appointment,   the   Director   shall   have   a   bachelor's   degree   from  a   recognized   college   or   university,   with   specialized   training   in   recreation   or   a   related   field,  and   at   least   four   (4)   years   of   experience   in   public   recreation   administration.   The  requirement   of   a   bachelor's   degree   may   be   waived   if   a   candidate   for   the   position   of  Director   has   at   least   fifteen   (15)   years   experience   and   training   in   the   field   of   public  recreation.  b.   At   any   time   wherein   the   position   of   Director   of   Recreation   and   Cultural   Affairs   is   vacant  the   duties   of   the   Director   of   Recreation   and   Cultural   Affairs,   in   part   or   in   total,   may   be  performed   by   a   Deputy   Department   Head   of   Recreation.   He/she   shall   receive   such  compensation as may be fixed by ordinance.  Any   references   within   this   section   to   a   Recreation   Director   shall   mean   the   Director   of  Recreation   and   Cultural   Affairs   or   a   Deputy   Department   Head   of   Recreation   as   the  Director of Recreation and Cultural Affairs.  (1972 Code § 2-12.1; Ord. No. 888-87 § 1; Ord. No. 2307-11 § II)  2-17.2   Residency.  The   Director   of   Recreation   shall   be   a   bona   fide   resident   of   the   Township.   A   bona   fide   resident,  for   the   purpose   of   this   section,   is   a   person   having   a   permanent   domicile   within   the   Township   and  one   which   has   not   been   adopted   with   the   intention   of   again   taking   up   or   claiming   a   previous  residence   outside   the   Township   limits.   The   Director   need   not   be   a   full   time   resident   at   the   time   of  appointment   but   shall   establish   bona   fide   residence   in   the   Township   within   one   (1)   year   of  appointment or be dismissed from office. (1972 Code § 2-12.2; Ord. No. 888-87 § 2)  2-17.3   Advisory Committee; Program.  The   Mayor   shall   appoint   a   Citizen's   Advisory   Committee   on   Recreation   to   advise   and   consult  with   the   Superintendent.   With   the   aid   and   assistance   of   the   Committee,   the   Director   shall   plan  and   develop   recreational   facilities;   promote   and   direct   an   all   year   program   of   cultural   and   leisure  time   activities   for   children,   youth   and   adults;   provide   for   the   optimum   coordination   and  development of public and private recreational facilities and programs. (1972 Code § 2-12.3)  2-17.4   Department; Functions.  The Department of Recreation and Cultural Affairs shall:  a.   Administer   and   operate   parks,   playgrounds   and   playfields   and   facilities   for   indoor   and  outdoor sports, athletic and recreational programs and activities for children and adults.  b.   Sponsor   and   administer   cultural   and   recreational   programs   and   activities   in   cooperation  with   other   public   and   private   agencies   and   organizations,   and   provide   specialized  programs for the aged, the handicapped and other special purposes.

c.   Employ   and   instruct   the   Department   of   Public   Works   with   respect   to   the   maintenance   and  repair   of   public   buildings   and   grounds   used,   controlled   or   managed   for   recreational  purposes by the Division.  d.   Establish   and   administer   a   community   school   program   whereby   school   properties   are   used  for   community   activities.   The   Department   of   Recreation   shall   be   assisted   by   a   Community  School   Advisory   Board   of   Trustees   separate   and   distinct   from   the   Recreation   Advisory  Board,   the   membership   of   which   shall   be   comprised   of   two   (2)   members   appointed   by  each   councilperson   and   two   (2)   members   appointed   by   the   Mayor,   each   member   to   serve  for one (1) year from date of appointment and until his successor is appointed.  (1972   Code   §   2-12.4;   Ord.   No.   363-75   §   1;   Ord.   No.   390-76  § 1; Ord. No. 478-78, A 1)  2-17.5   Regulations.  The   following   acts   are   expressly   prohibited   and/or   regulated   by   the   Recreation   Department   and  shall   require   enforcement   under   Chapter   IV.   The   following   such   acts   are   prohibited   and/or  regulated:  a.   Other   than   in   regulated   parking   areas,   no   vehicle   shall   be   allowed   within   any   park   owned  by   the   Township   or   under   its   jurisdiction   at   any   time   except   for   emergency   vehicles   or  others with written permission from the Recreation Director.  b.   It   shall   be   strictly   prohibited   for   one   to   start   or   maintain   a   fire   of   any   kind   or   to   use  barbecue   equipment   of   any   kind   in   any   park   owned   by   the   Township   or   under   its  jurisdiction or on other Township owned or maintained property.  c.   It   shall   be   strictly   prohibited   for   one   to   operate   a   powered   flight   model   in   any   park   owned  by   the   Township   or   under   its   jurisdiction   or   on   other   Township   owned   or   maintained  property.  d.   It   shall   be   unlawful   for   a   person   to   enter   into   any   park   owned   by   the   Township   or   under   its  jurisdiction   or   on   other   Township   owned   or   maintained   property   and   post   bills   of   any   sort  without the express permission of the Recreation Director.  e.   A   permit   shall   be   required   for   organized   group/team   practices ,   and   games   and/or  activities .   All   permits   are   issued   through   the   Recreation   Department.   This   shall   include  all   recreational   facilities   and   open   space   located   within   the   parks   and   playgrounds   deemed  to   be   under   the   jurisdiction   of   the   Recreation   Department.   The   Recreation   Department  shall   establish   reasonable   guidelines,   policies,   and   prohibitions   for   the   issuance   of   said  permits.  f.   Private   businesses   shall   be   prohibited   from   using   any   municipal   recreational   facility  without   the   written   permission   of   the   Recreation   Department.   The   use   of   all   recreational  facilities   under   the   jurisdiction   of   the   Recreation   Department   for   financial   gain   by  businesses,   organizations,   and   individuals   shall   be   prohibited   without   the   written  permission of the Recreation Department.  g.   Scholastic   games   and   practices   shall   have   priority   over   all   other   users   including   those   who  may have previously occupied the park area.

h.   There   shall   be   no   private   tennis   lessons   held   on   the   tennis   courts   within   the   Township  parks.   (Redundant with letter f)  i h .   Bike   riding ,   and   roller   blading ,   skateboarding   and   the   use   of   any   and   all   riding   devices  utilizing   wheels   or   rollers   are   prohibited   on   all   hard   court   surfaces   within   any   Recreation  facility.   are prohibited on the tennis courts within any Township park.  j i .   People   are   prohibited   from   wearing   hard   sole   shoes   on   the   tennis   courts   within   any  Township   park.   Additionally,   the   use   of   metal   spiked   or   cleated   footwear   is   prohibited   on  all artificial or synthetic surfaces.  k j .   There   shall   be   one   (1)   hour   time   limit   for   the   use   of   the   tennis   courts   when   other   players  are waiting to use the tennis courts.  l k .   The   Recreation   Department   may   charge   and   collect   in   advance   a   reasonable   fee   for  admission   to   or   use   of   facilities,   programs,   or   activities   as   specifically   authorized   by  Resolution of Council.  l .   Golfing activities are prohibited in all Recreation Department facilities.  m .   Abusive language and actions are prohibited in all Recreation Department facilities .  n .   The   possession,   use,   and/or   consumption   of   alcohol   is   prohibited   in   all   Recreation  Department facilities without the written permission of the Recreation Director.  o .   Smoking   of   cigarettes   and   use   of   tobacco   products   within   or   adjacent   to   public   parks ,  playgrounds   and   ballfields   under   the   jurisdiction   of   the   Recreation   Department   is  prohibited.  p .   Excessive noise is prohibited at all times.  q .   All   dogs   must   be   leashed   within   the   confines   of   all   Recreation   Department   facilities.  Furthermore,   dogs   shall   be   prohibited   from   being   inside   the   confines   of   a   K iddie   P ark ,   as  defined herein .  r .   No   littering   or   dumping   is   permitted.   Dumpsters   located   within   all   Recreation   Department  facilities are restricted to municipal use only .  s .   No   person   shall   conduct   or   engage   in   any   activity   that   is   either   a   prohibited   activity   or   an  activity that may endanger the health, safety or welfare of others.  (1972 Code § 2-12.5; Ord. No. 1722-00 § 1)  2-17.6   Public Parks; Hours and Regulations.  a.   The   following   public   parks   of   the   Township,   and   the   parking   areas   located   within   or  adjacent   thereto,   shall   be   closed   to   the   public   and   for   parking   of   any   vehicles   between   the  hours of 10:00 p.m. and 6:00 a.m. prevailing time , unless otherwise posted .  1.   Byrne Memorial Park;

2.   Colgate Field;  3.   Degnan Memorial Field;  4.   Stagg Field;  5.   Lafayette Playground;  6.   William E. Boland Park;  7.   O'Connor Park;  8.   Jenkins Playground;  9.   Rolling Green Tennis Courts;  10.   Minish Park;  11.   Gilbert Place;  12.   Club Boulevard Playground ;  13.   Ridgeway Avenue Park. .  Any   and   all   future   parks   under   the   jurisdiction   of   the   Municipal   government   of   the  Township of West Orange.  b.   It   shall   be   unlawful   for   any   person   to   enter   in   or   upon,   occupy,   be   present   in   or   park   any  vehicle,   or   suffer   or   permit   to   be   parked   any   vehicle   in   the   aforesaid   public   parks,   or  parking   areas   located   in   or   adjacent   thereto,   between   the   hours   of   10:00   p.m.   and   6:00  a.m.,   prevailing   time,   and   it   shall   be   unlawful   for   any   person   or   persons   to   at   any   time  remove,   damage,   mutilate,   deface   or   destroy   any   barricade,   chain,   notice,   sign   or   other  object   used   to   block   the   entrance   to   the   aforesaid   public   parks   or   parking   areas,   or  indicating that the parks or parking areas are closed.  c.   Nothing herein contained shall be deemed or construed to apply to:  1.   Personnel   of   the   Township   engaged   in   or   conducting   Township   business   in   any   public  park or parking area.  2.   Any   group   or   organization   holding   a   permit   to   conduct   an   activity   in   such   park   or  parking area for a period specifically prohibited.  3.   Any   person   found   in   a   park   or   parking   area   by   reason   of   an   unusual   or   unforeseen  emergency.  d.   There   are   areas   within   each   park   listed   in   subsection  2-17.6a,   except   for   Rolling   Green   Tennis   Courts,   that   contain   playground   apparatus.   These  areas   are   considered   "Kiddie   Parks."   "Kiddie   Parks"   are   to   be   used   by   children   under   the  age of twelve (12) years old and under the supervision of an adult.  e.   It   shall   be   unlawful   for   any   person,   regardless   of   age,   to   enter   in   or   upon,   occupy,   be  present   in   or   park   any   vehicle,   or   suffer   or   permit   to   be   parked   any   vehicle   in   any   of   the  "Kiddie Parks," or the parking area located in or adjacent thereto, after   8:00   10:00   p.m.  (1972 Code § 2-12.6; Ord. No. 313-74, S 1; Res. 2416-76; New; Ord. No. 1722-00 § 2)

2-17.7   Golf Prohibited.  a.   Prohibition.   The   playing   and/or   practicing   of   golf   in   parks   owned   by   the   Township   or  under   its   jurisdiction   or   on   other   Township   owned   or   maintained   property   is   expressly  prohibited at all times.  b.   Penalties.   Any   person   convicted   of   violating   this   subsection   shall   be   fined   twenty-five  ($25.00)   dollars   for   a   first   offense   in   addition   to   any   court   costs.   Conviction   of   a   second  offense   shall   result   in   a   fine   of   fifty   ($50.00)   dollars   in   addition   to   any   court   costs.  Conviction   of   a   third   or   subsequent   offense   shall   result   in   a   fine   of   one   hundred   ($100.00)  dollars   for   each   such   offense   in   addition   to   any   court   costs.   There   shall   be   a   mandatory  court appearance for any summons charging a third or subsequent offense.  (Ord. No. 1521-98 §§ 1, 2)  2-17. 8   7   Identification   Cards;   Fees Use   of   Facilities   by   Non-Residents ;   Rules   and  Regulations; Exceptions.  a.   Township   owned   playgrounds,   fields   and   other   recreation   facilities   shall   only   be   utilized  by   bona   fide   residents   of   West   Orange.   All   persons,   twelve   (12)   years   of   age   or   older,  using   such   facilities   must   have   in   their   possession   a   Township   issued   identification   card  which shall be issued for a period of five (5) years and must be renewed thereafter.  b.   The   Department   of   Recreation   shall   promulgate   rules   and   regulations   designed   to   carry  out the intent and purpose of this subsection.  c.   The   provisions   of   this   subsection   shall   not   apply   to   any   recreation   facility   wholly   or  partially constructed or improved with New Jersey Green Acres trust funds.  (Ord. No. 1527-98)  2-17. 9 8   Registration Policy for Participation in All Recreation Programs.  Each   and   every   time   a   West   Orange   resident   completes   a   registration   form   for   participation   in   a  Township   of   West   Orange   Recreation   Program,   the   resident   must   provide   documents   to  demonstrate   (i)   Parent/guardian   proof   of   identity   and   residency   (ii)   Child's   proof   of   identity   and  school enrollment status.  a.   Proof   of   parent/guardian   identity   and   residency   by   providing   one   of   the   following  documents:  1.   Valid   New   Jersey   Division   of   Motor   Vehicle   Driver's   License   showing   a   West  Orange address;  2.   Valid   New   Jersey   Division   of   Motor   Vehicles   ID   Card   showing   a   West   Orange  address;  3.   Valid West Orange Recreation Department ID Card.  b.   Child's Documentation. Both of the following documents must be provided:  1.   Child's birth certificate; and  2.   Child's most recent report card.

c.   Proof   of   Custodial   Situations.   Legal   and   binding   court   documents   and/or   a  correspondence   or   document   signed   by   an   attorney-at-law   attesting   to   the   custodial  situation shall constitute sufficient proof of the custodial situation.  (Ord. No. 2148-07 § II; Ord. No. 2219-09 § II)  2-17. 9   Penaltie s  Any   person   or   entity   that   violat es   any   of   the   provisions   of   Sections   2-17. 5   through   2-  17.9   or   any   rule   or   regulation   expounded   pursuant   hereto   shall   be   subject   to   the   replacement,  repair   or   restoration   of   any   damaged   park   or   recreation   area   property   and   shall   be   liable   to   a  penalty as follows:  a.   First offense: twenty-five dollars   ($25) .  b.   Second offense:   fifty   dollars   ($ 50 ) .  c.   Third offense:   one hundred   dollars   ($ 100 ) .  2-18 - 2-25   RESERVED.  4815-6718-7228, v. 1 4843-8947-7917, v. 1

2427-14  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance creates three on street handicapped parking spaces, known as  Restricted Parking Spaces under N.J.S.A 39:4-197.5, to assist two residents with   D isabled  P ersons   I dentifications issued by the New Jersey Motor Vehicle Commission. One of the  spaces will be created on the westerly side of Longview Street, a public street within the Hutton  Lafayette Garden Apartment Complex, in the vicinity of number 63, and the other two spaces will  be created on Ashland Avenue just north of Park Drive in the vicinity of the apartment building at  31-33 Park Avenue where the other resident resides. These stalls will also serve the  Physician ’ s office at   35   Park Avenue.   Both these properties are corner lots with frontage along  Ashland Avenue.   These three stalls will not be Restricted   P arking Zones established under  Subsection 7.32.2 pursuant to N.J.S.A. 39:4-197.5 which are on street handicapped parking  stalls established in front of a residence for the exclusive use of   a resident of that home with a  D isabled   P ersons   I dentification. A complex of multifamily buildings prevents this on Longview  Street and the overnight parking prohibition on Park Avenue does not make it practical to place  such a stall there. These stalls once created, while assisting residen ts   of 63 Longview Street  and 31-33 Park Drive, will be available for anyone with a NJMVC   D isabled   P ersons   I dentification  with a corresponding special vehicle identification card.

Subsection 7-23.1, Turn Prohibitions, of the Revised General Ordinances of the  Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows: ( italic type denotes deletion,  bold type denotes addition):  Intersection   Turn   Hours/   Movement Prohibited  Prohibited   Days  Northfield Avenue   Left   All   Eastbound on Northfield  and Glen View Drive   Avenue to northbound on  Glen View Drive  Section 2.   If any provisions of this Ordinance or application thereof , under any  circumstances, is held invalid, the invalidity shall not affect any of the provisions or applications of this  Ordinance that can be given effect without the valid provision(s) or application and to this end, the  provisions of this Ordinance are severable.  Section 3.   All other Ordinances or parts of Ordinances inconsistent with this Ordinance are  hereby repealed.  Section 4.
