2428-14  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance will prohibit left turns from Northfield Avenue eastbound onto Glen View  Drive   nor t h bound. Glen View Drive is located on the northerly side of Northfield Avenue just  east of the signalized intersection of Northfield Avenue and Old Short Hills Road. Due to the  presence of the traffic signal ,   westbound traffic along Northfield Avenue will often queue up to  and through the intersection of Northfield Avenue and Glen View Drive. Here Northfield Avenue  contains three travel lanes: two thru lanes and one exclusive left turn lane onto Old Short Hills  R oad. The volume of westbound traffic coupled with three lanes makes this left turn from  Northfield Avenue onto Glen View Drive difficult .   Vehicles waiting to turn onto Glen View Drive  from Northfield Avenue eastbound   block of one of the two eastbound lanes on Northfield  Avenue   which then delays eastbound traffic . This turn prohibition will result in the safe and  efficient movement of traffic. Residents of Glen View Drive will still be able to directly access  their homes from Northfield Avenue   we stbound. In the   ea stbound direction they will need to turn  left at Herbert Terrace, proceed to Beacon Place th e n Glen View Drive. From Old Short Hills  R oad they shall proceed westbound on Northfield Avenue to Herbert Terrace.

pages.text: 142-14  August 12, 2014  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   Alice   Beirne,   Esq.,   has   served   as   the   Zoning   Board   of   Adjustment   ( “ Zoning  Board ” )   Attorney   in   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” ),   pursuant   to   Chapter   25,   Section  47.6 of the Township ’ s Revised General Ordinances, since 2006; and  WHERAS,   in   her   capacity   as   Zoning   Board   Attorney,   Ms.   Beirne   has   exhibited   special   skill  and expertise in defending the Zoning Board against legal challenges to its determinations; and  WHEREAS,   on   June   5,   2014,   a   Complaint   in   Lieu   of   Prerogative   Writs   was   filed   in   the   matter  captioned   Laura   Gavilanez   v.   Zoning   Board   of   Adjustment   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange ,   Docket  No. ESX-L-4039-14 (the   “ Gavilanez   Litigation ” ); and  WHEREAS,   in   the   Gavilanez   Litigation,   the   plaintiff   is   challenging   the   Zoning   Board ’ s   April  17,   2014   denial   of   her   application   to   convert   the   first   floor   apartment   of   her   two-family   dwelling  located   at   134   Main   Street,   designated   as   Block   89,   Lot   32   on   the   Tax   Maps   of   the   Township,   into   a  hair   salon,   a   personal-service   use ,   with   the   second   floor   apartment   remaining   as   a   residential   use;  and  WHEREAS,   the   Law   Department   recommends   that   Ms.   Beirne   be   authorized   to   file   an  answer and defend the Zoning Board in the   Gavilanez   Litigation at an hourly rate of $150; and  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange,   that   Alice   Beirne,   Esq.   is   hereby   authorized   to   file   an   answer   and   defend   the   Zoning   Board  in the   Gavilanez   Litigation at an hourly rate of $150; and it is further  RESOLVED,   that   this   Resolution   shall   be   published   and   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   Office  for reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J.   Carnevale   Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   August 12, 2014

pages.text: 143-14  August 12, 2014  Resolution Requesting Approval of Items of Revenue and Appropriation  NJS 40A:4-87  WHEREAS, NJS 40A:4-87 provides that the Director of the Division of Local  Government  Services may approve the insertion of any special item of revenue   in the budget of any  county or   municipality when such item shall have been made available by law and the  amount thereof was  not determined at the time of the adoption of the budget; and  WHEREAS, the Director may also approve the insertion of an appropriation for the equal  amount;  NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED, that the   Township Council   of the Township of  West Orange in the County of Essex, New Jersey, hereby requests the Director of the  Division of Local Government Services to approve the insertion of an item of revenue in  the budget of the year 201 4   in the sum of $ 1 , 250 .00 , which is now available as a  revenue from   FY2013 Emergency Management   CERT Trailer Grant , p ursuant to the  provision of the statute, and  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the like sum of   $1,250.00 is hereby appropriated  under the caption   FY2013 Emergency Management   CERT Trailer Grant ; and  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the above is a result of a State grant of   $1,250.00  from   FY2013 Emergency Management   CERT Trailer Grant . #2111165 v5  105312-53691  145-14  August 12, 2014  RESOLUTION   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE   DIRECTING  THE   UNDERTAKING   OF   A   CONTINUING   DISCLOSURE   REVIEW   AND  AUTHORIZING   PARTICIPATION   IN   THE   MUNICIPALITIES  CONTINUING   DISCLOSURE   COOPERATION   INITIATIVE   OF   THE  DIVISION   OF   ENFORCEMENT   OF   THE   U .S.   SECURITIES   AND  EXCHANGE COMMISSION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Governmental   Entity ” )   has   entered   into  continuing   disclosure   agreement ( s )   in   connection   with   certain   of   its   prior   bond   and/or   note   issuance ( s )  (the   “ Prior   Issuances ” ) ,   agreeing   to   file   certain   financial   information   and   operating   data   and/or   certain  enumerated   event   notices   with   the   former   nationally   recognized   municipal   securities   information  repositories   or   the   Municipal   Securities   Rulemaking   Board   (the   “ MSRB ” )   pursuant   to   the   provisions   of  Rule 15c2-12 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the   “ Rule ” ) ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Governmental   Entity   has   made   certain   representations   regarding   it s   continuing  disclosures in   bond and note   offering documents in connection with   its Prior Issuances ; and  WHEREAS,   in   response   to   widespread   concerns   that   many   municipal   issuers   have   not   been  complying   with   their   obligations   to   file   continuing   disclosure   documents   under   the   Rule,   and   furthermore  have   made   false   representations   concerning   compliance   in   bond   and   note   offering   documents,   the  Division   of   Enforcement   (the   “ Division ” )   of   the   U.S.   Securities   and   Exchange   Commission   (the  “ Commission ” )   has   set   forth   its   Municipalities   Continuing   Disclosure   Cooperation   Initiative   (the   “ MCDC  Initiative ” ) ,   attached   hereto   as   Exhibit   A,   whereby   t he   Commission   will   recommend   favorable   settlement  terms   to   municipal   issuers   involved   in   the   offer   or   sale   of   municipal   securities,   as   well   as   underwriters   of  such   offerings,   if   they   self-report   to   the   Division   possible   violations   involving   materially   inaccurate  statements   in   bond   and   note   offering   documents   relating   to   prior   compliance   with   continuing   disclosure  obligations pursuant to the Rule;   and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   the   Local   Finance   Notice   2014- 9  ,   attached   hereto   as   Exhibit   B ,   the   Director  of   the   Local   Finance   Board,   Division   of   Local   Government   Services   of   the   Department   of   Community  Affairs   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   has   recommended   the   undertaking   of   a   Review   (as   hereinafter   defined)  by   all   municipal   issuers   and   participation   in   the   MCDC   Initiative ,   where   appropriate ,   as   determined   by  the   facts of each   Review   (the   “ LFB Recommendation ” );  NOW, THEREFORE,   IN CONNECTION WITH THE LFB RECOMMENDATION,   BE IT RESOLVED  BY THE   GOVERNMENTAL ENTITY , as follows:  Section   1.   The   Governmental   Entity ,   through   its   Chief   Financial   Officer   or   through   the  engagement   of   a   third-party   disclosure-dissemination   agent ,   is   hereby   directed   to   conduct   a   continuing  disclosure   review   of   its   prior   continuing   disclosure   undertakings   (the   “ Review ” ) ,   and   the   Governmental  Entity   hereby   ratif ies   any   such   previously   conducted   Review .   Such   Review   shall   include,   but   is   not  limited   to,   a   historical   review   of   the   Governmental   Entity ’ s   continuing   disclosure   obligations   and   filings  in   connection   with   its   Prior   Issuances   that   are   presently   outstanding   and   which   are   no   longer   outstanding  but,   as   of   the   date   five   years   prior   to   the   date   of   submission   of   the   Questionnaire   (as   hereinafter   defined) ,

#2111165 v5  105312-53691  were   outstanding;   and   the   undertaking ,   at   any   time,   of   any   applicable   remedial   filings   with   the   MSRB  deemed necessary for compliance with its continuing disclosure obligations .  Section   2.   T he   Governmental   Entity ,   through   its   Chief   Financial   Officer,   is   hereby   authorized   to  participate   in   the   MCDC   Initiative,   if   in   the   discretion   of   the   Chief   Financial   Officer   after   consultation  with   Governmental   Entity   officials,   it   is   determined   that   the   Governmental   Entity   may   have   made  materially   inaccurate   statements   in   its   bond   and   note   offering   documents   relating   to   prior   compliance   with  continuing   disclosure   obligations   pursuant   to   the   Rule   in   connection   with   its   Prior   Issuances   issued   during  the period   beginning   five years   prior to the date of submission of the Questionnaire .  Section   3.   The   Chief   Financial   Officer   of   the   Governmental   Entity   is   hereby   authorized   to  execute   and   deliver   any   and   all   documents   and   instruments,   including   the   Municipalities   Continuing  Disclosure   Cooperation   Initiative   Questionnaire   for   Self-Reporting   Entities   contained   in   the   MCDC  Initiative   (the   “ Questionnaire ” ) ,   and   to   do   and   cause   to   be   done   any   and   all   acts   and   things   necessary   or  proper   for   participating   in   the   MCDC   Initiative   and   all   related   transactions ,   including   the   Review ,  contemplated by this resolution.  Section   4.   All   resolutions   or   proceedings,   or   parts   thereof,   in   conflict   with   the   provisions   of   this  resolution are to the extent of such conflict hereby repealed.  Section 5. This resolution shall become effective in accordance with applicable law.  RECORDED VOTE :  AYE   NO   ABSTAIN  ABSENT  Karen J.   Carnevale   Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted: August 12, 2014

pages.text: 146-14  August 12, 2014  Resolution Requesting Approval of Items of Revenue and Appropriation  NJS 40A:4-87  WHEREAS, NJS 40A:4-87 provides that the Director of the Division of Local  Government  Services   may approve the insertion of any special item of revenue   in the budget of any county  or   municipality when such item shall have been made available by law and the amount thereof  was  not determined at the time of the adoption of the budget; and  WHEREAS, the Director may also approve the insertion of an appropriation for the equal  amount;  NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED, that the   Township Council   of the Township of West  Orange in the County of Essex, New Jersey, hereby requests the Director of the Division of  Local Government Services to approve the insertion of an item of revenue in the budget of the  year 201 4   in the sum of $ 5,000.00 , which is now available as a revenue from   FY201 4   Drive  Sober or Get Pulled Over Labor Day Crackdown Grant , p ursuant to the provision of the statute,  and  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the like sum of   $ 5,000. 00   is hereby appropriated under the  caption   FY201 4   Drive Sober or Get Pulled Over Labor Day Crackdown Grant ; and  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the above is a result of a State grant of   $ 5 , 000 .00   from  FY201 4   Drive Sober or Get Pulled Over Labor Day Crackdown Grant .  Karen J.   Carnevale   Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted: August 12, 2014

147-14  August 12, 2014  R esolution  Supporting the   Dri ve Sober or Get Pulled Over  2014   Statewide   Crackdown  Whereas   , impaired drivers on our nation ’ s roads kill someone every 30 minutes,  50 people per day, and almost 18,000 people each year; and  Whereas   , 19 % of motor vehicle fatalities in New Jersey   in 2012   we re alcohol-  related; and  Whereas   , a n   enforcement crackdown is planned to combat impaired driving; and  Whereas   , the   summer   season   and the Labor Day holiday in particular   are  traditionally   time s   o f social gatherings which   include alcohol; and  Whereas   , the State of New Jersey, Division of Highway Traffic Safety, has asked  law enforcement agencies throughout   the state   to participate in the   Dri ve Sober  or Get Pulled Over 2014   Statewide   Crackdown   ; and  Whereas   , the project will involve increased impaired dri ving enforcement from  August 15   through   September 1, 2014 ; and  Whereas   , an increase in impaired driving enforcement and a reduction in  impaired driving will save lives on our roadways;  Therefore   , be is resolve d that the TOWNSHIP OF WEST   ORANGE   declares its  support for the   Dri ve Sober or Get Pulled Over 2014   Statewide   Crackdown  from   August 15 through September 1, 2014   and pledges to increase awareness  of the dangers of drinking and driving.  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney, Council President  Adopted: August 12, 2014

pages.text: 149-14  August 12, 2014  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Housing   Rehabilitation   Program   has   a  mortgage   against   certain   real   property   owned   by   Cristian   Pena   and   Belkis   Pena   (the   “ Owner s ” )  residing   at   2 83   Saint   Cloud   Avenue ,   Block   1 68 ,   Lot   1 0.02   (the   “ Property ” ),   in   the   amount   of  F ifteen   Thousand   Dollars   ($15,000 .00),   which   was   dated   January   11 ,   20 13   and   recorded   in   the  Essex   County   Register ’ s   Office   on   May   24 ,   20 13   in   Book   1 2347 ,   Page   6 11   (the   “ WOHRP  Mortgage ” ); and  WHEREAS,   there   is   a   first   mortgage   on   the   Property   from   Wells   Fargo   Home   Mortgage  Corporation   ( “ Wells   Fargo ” )   in   the   outstanding   amount   of   One   Hundred   and   Ninety-Nine  Thousand,   One   Hundred   and   Five   Dollars   and   Eight   Cents   ($ 1 99 , 105 . 08 )   (the   “ First   Mortgage ” );  and  WHEREAS,   the   Owner s   desires   to   refinance   t he i r   home   and   obtain   a   new   mortgage  loan   from   Wells   Fargo   in   an   amount   not   to   exceed   Two   Hundred   and   Two   Thousand,   Two  Hundred   and   Eighty-Six   Dollars   and   Thirty-Two   Cents   ($ 202 , 286 . 32 ),   and   with   an   interest   rate  of   4.25 %   (the   “ New Mortgage ” ); and  WHEREAS,   the New   Mortgage will pay off the First   Mortgage; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Owner   has   requested   that   the   Township   of   West   Orange   subordinate  the WOHRP Mortgage to the New Mortgage; and  WHEREAS ,   in   connection   with   the   New   Mortgage,   Wells   Fargo   has   accepted   that   the  fair   m arket   value   of   the   Property   is   approximately   Three -Hundred   and   Eighty -Eight   Thousand  D ollars   ($ 388 , 000 .00 )   based   on   the   ad   valorem   tax   information   upon   which   taxes   have   been  assessed ; and  WHEREAS,   based   on   the   amount   of   the   New   Mortgage   and   the   appraisal   accepted   by  Wells   Fargo ,   the   proposed   subordination   will   not   change   the   equity   available   to   satisfy   the  WOHRP Mortgage;

NOW   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Mayor   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   execute   a  Subordination   of   Mortgage   in   favor   of   Wells   Fargo   with   respect   to   the   Property   and   the   Owner s ;  and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Municipal   Clerk   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   attest   to   the  Mayor ’ s signature on the Subordination of Mortgage.  Karen   J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney ,   Council President  Adopted:   August 12, 2014  4814-6276-0220, v. 1

150-14  August 12, 2014  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   The   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   wishes   to   extend   the  agreement   with   the   New   Jersey   Medical   School   (NJMS)   Global   Tuberculosis   Institute   at  Rutgers   to   provide   TB   Physician   and   Medical   Consultation   Services   to   the   Township   Health  Department   for   an   additional   one   year   term   beginning   January   1,   2014   and   terminating  December 31,   2014 ; and  WHEREAS,   this   Eighth   Amendment   to   the   original   agreement   dated   April   1,   2005  between   UMDNJ   and   the   Township   will   allow   the   NJMS   Global   Tuberculosis   Institute   to  continue   to   provide   medical   consultation   services   in   connection   with   the   Tuberculosis  Prevention and Control Program as mandated by the State Department of Health; and  WHEREAS,   it   is   in   the   best   interest   of   the   Township   to   extend   the   terms   of   the  agreement   with   NJMS   for   the   period   of   January   1,   2014   through   December   31,   2014   to   meet  state mandated services for Tuberculosis;  WHEREAS,   annexed   hereto   as   Attachment   “ A ”   is   a   copy   of   the   proposed   Eighth  Amendment   to   the   Agreement,   and   annexed   hereto   as   Attachment   “ B ”   is   the   original  Agreement,   which   collectively   set   forth   the   terms   under   which   the   Township   will   receive   and  pay for said services;  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange,   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New   Jersey,   that   the   Mayor   and   Township   Clerk  are   authorized   to   execute   and   attest   to,   respectively,   the   Eighth   Amendment   to   the  agreement   with   the   New   Jersey   Medical   School   Global   Tuberculosis   Institute   at   Rutgers ,   for  the   period   between   January   1,   2014   and   December   31,   2014   at   the   annual   rate   of   $ 4,752.00  contingent   upon   the   availability   of   funds   to   be   appropriated   in   the   2014   Municipal   Budget ;  and

BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   be   published   as   required  by law.  Karen J. Carnevale   Susan McCartney  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   August 12, 2014  I hereby certify funds available :   01-2010-00-8632-210 - $1,500  01-2010-00-8752-200 - $2,800  12-6190-00-0220-020 -   $452  John O. Gross, CFO  4835-5322-9596, v. 1

documents.title: RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING EXECUTION OF METLIFE INSURANCE AGREEMENT

pages.text: 151-14  August 12, 2014  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   desires   to   offer   voluntary  insurance benefits to its full-time employees through a payroll deduction; and  WHEREAS,   MetLife   has   offered   to   undertake   a   voluntary   employee   enrollment   process  during   which   the   Township ’ s   full-time   employees   will   be   provided   with   information   explaining  available benefits packages and offered the opportunity to enroll; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   desires   to   enter   into   an   agreement   with   MetLife   (the  “ Agreement ” ),   a   copy   of   which   is   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A, ”   whereby   the   Township   shall  agree   to   provide   MetLife   with   access   to   its   full-time   employees   in   order   to   facilitate   the  aforementioned enrollment process; and  THEREFORE,   IT   IS   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   that   the   Township   Council   authorizes   the  Mayor to execute the attached Agreement; and  BE   IT   FURHTER   RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   and   supporting  documentation remain on file with the Municipal Clerk for inspection by the public.  Karen   J.   Carnevale   Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   August 12, 2014  4845-0061-6476, v. 1
E XHIBIT A

152-14  August 12, 2014  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS   ,   the Township of West Or ange retained Shirley M. Bishop , P.P., LLC  ( “ Bishop ” ) as a  housing and planning consultant in order to assist the Township with it s   obligations  under the  rules and regulations of the Council of Affordable Housing ( “ COAH ” ); and  WHEREAS   ,   the Township is now in the process   of preparing   its   third round Housing  Element  and Fair   Share Plan; and  WHEREAS   , Bishop has provided a proposal and budget indicating t hat the engagement  for  Providing   the above services shall be at a rate of $165.00 per hour for 2014 for an  amount not  to exceed $20,000.00; and  WHEREAS   , the Business Administrator recommends the continued engagement of  Bishop; and  WHEREAS   , the engagement of Bishop is a professional services contract   which   is  exempt from  Public   bidding requirements pursuant to   N.J.S.A.   40A:11-5(i ) ( a ) ( ii);  NOW, BE IT HEREBY RESOLVED   , by the Township Council of the Township of West  Orange that  the Township be and is hereby authorized to approve Bishop ’ s engagement for the  purposes of  assisting the Township in preparing   its   third round Housing Element and Fair Share Plan  a t  a   rate of $165.00 per hour   commencing August 1, 2014 through December 31, 2014 , for  an  amount not to exceed $20,000; and it is further  RESOLVED   , that the Mayor be and is hereby authorized to execute the proposed  agreement  with Bishop and the Municipal Clerk shall attest to the Mayor ’ s signature; and it is further

RESOLVED   , that the Municipal Clerk shall cause a copy of the contract to be available  for  inspection during normal business hours and publicized in accordance with applicable  law.  Karen   J.   Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney, Council President  Adopted: August 12, 2014

152-14  August 12, 2014  P ROFESSIONAL SERVICES AGREEMENT  Agreement made as of   , 2014 by and between the  Township of West Orange ( “ Township ” ) and Shirley M. Bishop, P.P. ( “ the Consultant ” ).  WHEREAS   , the Township is authorized by statute to appoint consultants and the  Consultant has agreed to continue to serve as housing consultant to the Township  pursuant to law and a professional services agreement has be en   authorized by  resolution of the Township.  NOW, THEREFORE   , it is agreed as follows:  1.   Retainer.   The Township retains the Consultant, and the Consultant agrees to  continue to provide housing consulting services to the Township in accordance  with this agreement.  2.   Scope of Services   .   The   Consultant will assist the Township in preparing   its  third round Housing Element and Fair Share Plan.  3.   Compensation   .   The compensation of the Consultant shall be at the rate of  $165.00 per hour   commencing August 1,   2014   through December 31, 2014   not to  exceed $20,000. The Consultant shall be reimbursed for actual out-of-pocket  expenses for travel, postage, duplication and communications.  IN WITNESS WHEREOF   ,   the parties have executed this agreement this  ___________day of  ______________, 2014.  Witness:   Township of West  Orange  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  Witness:  Shirley M. Bishop, P.P., LLC

154-14  August 12, 2014  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   NJSA 54:5-19.1   authorizes electronic tax sales pursuant to rules and  regulations to be promulgated by the Director of the Division of Local Government  Services, and  WHEREAS, the Director of the Division of Local Government Services has  promulgated rules and regulations for pilot programs, and  WHEREAS, the Director of the Division of Local Government Services has  approved NJ Tax lien Investors/Real Auction.com to conduct pilot programs, and  WHEREAS, the rules and regulations authorize a municipality to submit an  application for participation in the pilot program for an electronic tax sales, and  WHEREAS, an electronic tax sale is innovative and provides a greater pool of  potential lien buyers, thus creating the environment for a more complete tax sale  process, and  WHEREAS, the   Township of West Orange   wishes to participate in the pilot  program for an   E lectronic   T ax   S ale.  NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED,   by the governing body of the  Township of West Orange, in the State of   New Jersey, that the Tax Collector is  hereby authorized to complete an application to participate in the electronic tax  sale program and submit same to the Director of the Division of Local Government  Services.  Susan McCartney   Karen J. Carnevale  Council President   Municipal Clerk  Adopted: August 12, 2014

519877-2  155-14  August 12, 2014  RESOLUTION   AUTHORIZING   PARTICIPAT ION   IN THE  MIDDLESEX REGIONAL EDUCATIONAL SERVICES COMMISSION  (New Jersey State Approved Cooperative Pricing System   #65MCESCCPS)  “ SMALL TICKET LEASE PROGRAM ”  (Middlesex Regional Educational Services Commission B id No.   13/14-23)  Lessee:   Township of West Orange  Principal Amount Expected To Be Financed:   $ 9 0,923.50  Compound Period: Annual  Annual Rate: 2.271 %  WHEREAS,   the   Lessee   named   above   ( the   “ L essee ” )   is   a   political   subdivision   of   the   S tate   of   New  Jersey   ( the   “ State ” )   and   is   du l y   organize d   and   existing   pursuant   to   the   C onstitution   and   laws   of  the   State ;   and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   applicable   law,   the   Lessee   acting   through   its   governing   body   ( the  “ Governing   Body ” )   is   authorized   to   acquire   and   lease   personal   property   necessary   to   the  functions or operations of the Lessee ;   and  WHEREAS,   the   Governing   Body   hereby   finds   and   determines   the   execution   of   one   or   more   lease  -purchase   agreements   ( “ Equipment   Lease s ” )   in   the   principal   amount   not   exceeding   the   amount  stated   above   ( the   “ Principal   Amount ” )   for   the   purpose   of   acquiring   the   Equipment   described  generally   below   ( the   “ Equipment ” )   and   to   be   described   more   specifically   in   the   Equipment  Lease s   is appropriate and necessary to the functions and operations of the Lessee ;  Brief Descript ion of Equipment :   Two (2)   2014 Police Interceptor SUV  ________________________________________________________________________  WHEREAS ,   FIRST   HOPE   BANK,   a   National   Banking   Association ,   the   “ Lessor ” )   is   expected  to act as the Lessor under the Equipment Leases ;   and  WHEREAS,   the   Lessee   may   pay   certain   capital   expenditures   in   connection   with   the   Equipment  prior   to   its   receipt   of   proceeds   of   the   Equipment   Leases   ( “ Lease   Purchase   Proceeds ” )   for   such  expenditures ,   and such expenditures are not expected to exceed the Principal Amount ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Lessee   hereby   declare s   its   official   intent   to   be   reimbursed   for   any   capital  expenditures   made   for   the   Equipment   after   adoption   of   this   resolution   but   prior   to   the   issuance   of  the Equipment Leases   from the Lease Purchase Proceeds.  NOW, THEREFORE, Be It Resolved by the Governing Body of the Lessee   as follows :  Section   1.   The   Lessee   hereby   determines   that   it   has   critically   evaluated   the   financing   alternatives  and   that   entering   into   the   Equipment   Leases   and   financing   the   acquisition   of   the   Equipment  thereby is in the best interests of the Lessee.

519877-2  Section   2  .   The   Lessee   is   hereby   authorized   to   acquire   and   install   the   Equipment   and   is   hereby  authorized   to   finance   the   Equipment   by   entering   into   the   Equipment   Leases.   Any   action   taken   by  the Lessee   in connection therewith   is hereby   ratifi ed and confir med .  Section   3.   Either   one   of   the   Mayor   or   the   Business   Administrator   (each   an   “ Authorized  Representative ” )   acting   on   behalf   of   the   Lessee   is   hereby   authorized   to   negotiate,   enter   into,  execute,   and   deliver   one   or   more   Equipment   Leases   in   substantially   the   form   set   forth   in   the  document   presently   before   the   Governing   Body,   which   document   is   available   for   public  inspection   at   the   office   of   the   Lessee.   Each   Authorized   Representative   acting   on   behalf   of   the  Lessee   is   hereby   authorized   to   negotiate,   enter   into,   execute,   and   deliver   such   other   agreement   or  documents   relating   to   the   Equipment   Lease s   (including,   but   not   limited   to,   escrow   agreements)   as  the Authorized Representative deems necessary and appropriate.  Section   4 .   By   a   written   instrument   signed   by   any   Authorized   Representative,   said   Authorized  Representative   may   designate   specifically   identified   officers   or   employees   of   the   Lessee   to  execute   and   deliver   agreements   and   documents   relating   to   the   Equipment   Leases   on   behalf   of   the  Lessee.  Section   5  .   The   aggregate   original   principal   amount   of   the   Equipment   Leases   shall   not   exceed   the  Principal   Amount   and   shall   bear   interest   as   set   forth   in   the   Equipment   Leases   and   the   Equipment  Leases shall contain such options to purchase by the Lessee as set forth therein.  Section   6  .   The   Lessee ’ s   obligations   under   the   Equipment   Leases   shall   be   subject   to   annual  appropriation   or   renewal   by   the   Governing   Body   as   set   forth   in   each   Equipment   Lease s,   and   the  Lessee ’ s   obligations   under   the   Equipment   Leases   shall   not   constitute   indebtedness   of   the   L essee  under the   laws of the   State .  Section   7 .   It   is   hereby   determined   that   the   acquisition   of   the   Equipment   is   permitted   under   the  laws governing the Lessee   and is essential to the efficient operation of the Lessee .  Section   8 .   The   Governing   Body   of   the   Lessee   anticipates   that   the   Lessee   may   pay   certain   capital  expenditures   in   connection   with   the   Equipment   following   adoption   of   this   resolution   but   prior   to  the   receipt   of   the   Lease   Purchase   Proceeds   for   the   Equipment .   The   Governing   Body   of   the  Lessee   hereby   declares   the   Lessee ’ s   official   intent   to   use   the   Lease   Purchase   Proceeds   to  reimburse   itself   for   such   Equipment   expenditures.   This   section   of   the   r esolution   is   adopted   by   the  Governing   Body   of   the   Lessee   for   the   purpose   of   establishing   compliance   with   the   requirements  of   Section   1.150-2   of   Treasury   Regulations.   This   section   of   th is   r esolution   does   not   bind   the  Lessee   to   make   any   expenditure,   incur   any   obligation ,   or   proceed   with   the   acquisition   of   the  Equipment .  Section   9  .   The   Lessee   covenants   that   it   will   comply   with   all   requirements   of   the   Internal  Revenue   Code   of   1986,   as   amended,   (the   “ Code ” )   necessary   to   ensure   that   the   interest   portion   of  rental   payments   due   under   the   Equipment   Leases   will   be   excluded   from   gross   income   under  Section   103(a)   of   the   Code.   [ T he   Lessee   reasonably   expects   it   will   not   issue   more   than  $10,000,000   of   tax-exempt   obligations   (other   than   “ private   activity   bonds ”   that   are   not   “ qualified  501(c)(3)   bonds ” )   during   the   calendar   year   in   which   each   of   the   Equipment   Lease s   i s   issued   and  hereby   designates   each   Equipment   Lease   as   a   qualified   tax-exempt   obligation   for   pur poses   of  Section   265(b)   of   the   Code. ]   The   Authorized   Representative   is   further   authorized   to   take   such  actions   and   execute   such   documents   as   may   be   necessary   to   ensure   the   continued   status   of   the  interest   portion   of   the   rental   payments   due   on   the   Equipment   Leases   authorized   by   this   resolution

519877-2  as   excludable   from   gross   income   for   federal   income   tax   purposes   pursuant   to   Section   103 (a)   of  the Code .  Section   1 0 . This   r esolution shall take effect immediately upon its adoption and approval.  ADOPTED AND APPROVED on thi s ___________________, 201___ .  Susan McCartney   Karen J. Carnevale  Council President   Municipal Clerk  Adopted: August 12, 2014  CERTIFICATE  The   undersigned   Secretary/Clerk   of   the   above-named   Lessee   hereby   certifies   and   attests  that   (i)   the   undersigned   has   access   to   the   official   records   of   the   Governing   Body   of   the   Lessee ;  (ii)   the   foregoing   resolutions   were   duly   adopted   by   the   favorable   vote   of   the   members   of   the  Governing   Body   of   the   Lessee   at   a   [regular/special]   meeting   of   the   Governing   Body   duly   called,  regularly   convened   and   attended   throughout   by   the   requisite   quorum   of   the   member s   thereof;   (iii)  such   resolutions   have   not   been   amended   or   altered   and   are   in   full   force   and   effect   on   the   date  stated   below ;   and   (iv)   such   meeting   of   the   Governing   Body   relating   to   the   authorization   and  delivery   of   the   Equipment   Lease   has   been   conducted   in   accordance   with   the   Open   Public  Meetings Act , N.J.S.A. 10:4-6   et seq . ,   and   any   internal procedures of the Governing Body .  ______________________________________  Signature of Secretary/Clerk of Lessee  Print Name: ____________________________  Official Title: ___________________________  Date: __ _______________, 201 4

The   Governing   Body   of   the   Lessee   anticipates   that   the   Lessee   may   pay   certain   capital  expenditures   in   connection   with   the   Equipment   following   adoption   of   this   resolution   but   prior   to  the   receipt   of   the   Lease   Purchase   Proceeds   for   the   Equipment .   The   Governing   Body   of   the  Lessee   hereby   declares   the   Lessee ’ s   official   intent   to   use   the   Lease   Purchase   Proceeds   to  reimburse   itself   for   such   Equipment   expenditures.   This   section   of   the   r esolution   is   adopted   by   the  Governing   Body   of   the   Lessee   for   the   purpose   of   establishing   compliance   with   the   requirements  of   Section   1.150-2   of   Treasury   Regulations.   This   section   of   th is   r esolution   does   not   bind   the  Lessee   to   make   any   expenditure,   incur   any   obligation ,   or   proceed   with   the   acquisition   of   the  Equipment .  Section   9  .   The   Lessee   covenants   that   it   will   comply   with   all   requirements   of   the   Internal  Revenue   Code   of   1986,   as   amended,   (the   “ Code ” )   necessary   to   ensure   that   the   interest   portion   of  rental   payments   due   under   the   Equipment   Leases   will   be   excluded   from   gross   income   under  Section   103(a)   of   the   Code.   [ T he   Lessee   reasonably   expects   it   will   not   issue   more   than

519877-2  $10,000,000   of   tax-exempt   obligations   (other   than   “ private   activity   bonds ”   that   are   not   “ qualified  501(c)(3)   bonds ” )   during   the   calendar   year   in   which   each   of   the   Equipment   Lease s   i s   issued   and  hereby   designates   each   Equipment   Lease   as   a   qualified   tax-exempt   obligation   for   pur poses   of  Section   265(b)   of   the   Code. ]   The   Authorized   Representative   is   further   authorized   to   take   such  actions   and   execute   such   documents   as   may   be   necessary   to   ensure   the   continued   status   of   the  interest   portion   of   the   rental   payments   due   on   the   Equipment   Leases   authorized   by   this   resolution  as   excludable   from   gross   income   for   federal   income   tax   purposes   pursuant   to   Section   103 (a)   of  the Code .  Section   1 0 . This   r esolution shall take effect immediately upon its adoption and approval.  ADOPTED AND APPROVED on thi s ___________________, 201___ .  Susan McCartney   Karen J. Carnevale  Council President   Municipal Clerk  Adopted: August 12, 2014  CERTIFICATE  The   undersigned   Secretary/Clerk   of   the   above-named   Lessee   hereby   certifies   and   attests  that   (i)   the   undersigned   has   access   to   the   official   records   of   the   Governing   Body   of   the   Lessee ;  (ii)   the   foregoing   resolutions   were   duly   adopted   by   the   favorable   vote   of   the   members   of   the  Governing   Body   of   the   Lessee   at   a   [regular/special]   meeting   of   the   Governing   Body   duly   called,  regularly   convened   and   attended   throughout   by   the   requisite   quorum   of   the   member s   thereof;   (iii)  such   resolutions   have   not   been   amended   or   altered   and   are   in   full   force   and   effect   on   the   date  stated   below ;   and   (iv)   such   meeting   of   the   Governing   Body   relating   to   the   authorization   and  delivery   of   the   Equipment   Lease   has   been   conducted   in   accordance   with   the   Open   Public  Meetings Act , N.J.S.A. 10:4-6   et seq . ,   and   any   internal procedures of the Governing Body .  ______________________________________  Signature of Secretary/Clerk of Lessee  Print Name: ____________________________  Official Title: ___________________________  Date: __ _______________, 201_

documents.title: PURCHASE OF 4 POLICE VEHICLES THROUGH MCCPC 2014

pages.text: 158-14  August 12, 2014  RESOLUTION TO   PURCHASE FOUR   ( 4 ) 201 4   POLICE VEHICLES  THROUGH MORRIS COUNTY   COOPERATIVE   PRICING   COUNCIL  WHEREAS,   the West Orange Police Department must maintain a standard number  of   vehicles   to be   avail able for duty at all times. In order to maintain the fleet, new vehicles  are acquired while older ones are repurposed. The number of vehicles to be acquired  this year is f our ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   is   a   member   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:11-  11(6)   of   the   Morris   County   Cooperative   Pricing   Council   ( “ MCCPC ” )   and   as   a   Local  Government   U nit,   may   without   ad vertising   for   bids,   purchase   any   goods   or   services   under  contract with said Cooperative; and  WHEREAS,   the   MCCPC   has   through   competitive   bidding   awarded   a   contract   to   Beyer  Ford,   170   Ridgedale   Avenue,   Morristown,   New   Jersey   07936   for   various   vehicles   and   trucks;  and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   desires   to   authorize   the   purchase   of   three   (3)  2014   Police   Interceptor   SUV ’ s   with   options   MCCPC   Contract   #15A   Item   #3   in   the   amount   of  $45,461.75   each,   and   one   (1)   2014   Police   Interceptor   Sedan   AWD   with   options   MCCPC  Contract   #15A,   Item   #3   in   the   amount   of   $32,419.75  ,   resulting   in   the   total   cost   of   $168,805.00;  and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   is   a   member   of   the   Middlesex   Regional   Educational   Services  Commission ( “ MRESC ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   the   funding   of   the   above   mentioned   vehicles   will   be   financed   through   the  three   year   lease   purchase   program   offered   through   the   Middlesex   Regional   Educational   Services  Commission #65MCESCCPS.  NOW   BE   IT   RESOLVED,   by   the   Municipal   Council   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   hereby  authorizes   the   Purchasing   Agent   on   behalf   of   the   Township   to   issue   and   execute   a   purchase   order  in   the   amount   of   $161,283.00   to   Beyer   Ford   with   funds   certified   available   by   the   Chief   Finance  Officer.  Susan McCartney   Karen J. Carnevale  Council President   Municipal Clerk  Adopted: August 12, 2014  I hereby certify funds are available   from account:  John O. Gross , CFO

pages.text: 159-14  August 12, 2014  RESOLUTION  AUTHORIZING THE PURCHASE OF THREE (3) 2014 FORD F-450 4WD  T HROUGH NEW JERSEY STATE CONTRACT   #A83560  WHEREAS , the Township of West Orange   is in need of   replacing older trucks  used by the Department of Public   Works ; and  WHEREAS , the Township of West Orange as a Local Government Unit, may  without advertising for bids, purchase any goods or services under any contract for such  goods or services entered into on behalf of the State by the Division of Purchase and  Property in the Department of the   Treasury   pursuant   to the provision of N.J.S.A. 40A: 11  -12; and  WHEREAS , the State Division of Purchase and Property has awarded a contract  To Beyer Ford, 170 Ridgedale Avenue, Morristown, New Jersey 07936 for various  vehicles and trucks; and  WHEREAS , the Township of West Orange desires to authorize the purchase of  three (3) 2014 Ford F-450 4wd trucks with   F450 options as described in the State of New  Jersey Contract #A83560.   Each vehicle has a cost of $53,761.00, resulting in total cost of  $161,283.00.

NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED , by the Municipal Council of the Township  of West Orange hereby authorizes the Purchasing Agent, on behalf of the Township to  issue and execute a purchase order in the amount of $161,283.00 to Beyer Ford with  funds certified available by the Chief Finance Officer. 2414-14  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING  CHAPTER 7, TRAFFIC, SUBSECTION 7-20, STOP INTERSECTION OF  THE REVISED GENERAL ORDINANCES OF  THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  BE IT ORDAINED, by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, in the  County of Essex, State of New Jersey, that Marshall Street and Phyllis Road and Holmes Street  and Phyllis Road shall be designated as a Stop Intersections pursuant to N.J.S.A. 39:4-8(b) under  the Municipal Engineer ’ s Certification and License Number GE026706 as follows:  Section 1.   The Township Council of the Township of West Orange finds it in the  interest of public safety to designate Marshall Street and Phyllis Road and Holmes Street and  Phyllis Road as Stop Intersections pursuant to N.J.S.A.39:4-8(b).  Section 2.   The Municipal Engineer of the Township of West Orange has submitted  and certified all the legislative requirements pursuant to N.J.S.A. 39:4-8(b) and the criteria as set  forth by the New Jersey Department of Transportation, Bureau of Traffic Engineering and  Investigations have been met.  Section 3.   Subsection 7-20, Stop Intersections, of the Revised General Ordinances of  the Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows pursuant to the  provisions of N.J.S.A. 39:4-140 ( italic type denotes deletion,   bold type denotes addition) :  Intersection   Stop Signs On:  Marshall   Street and Phyllis Road   Marshall Street  Holmes Street and Phyllis Road   Holmes Street  Section 4.   If any provisions of this Ordinance or application thereof, under any  circumstances, is held invalid, the invalidity shall not affect any of the provisions or applications  of this Ordinance that can be given effect without the valid provision(s) or application and to this

end, the provisions of the Ordinance are severable.  Section 5.   All other Ordinances or parts of Ordinances inconsistent with this  Ordinance are hereby repealed.  Section 6.   Unless another penalty is expressly provided by New Jersey Statute, every  person convicted of a violation of any provision of this Ordinance or any supplement thereto  shall be liable to the penalty of not more that fifty dollars ($50.00) or imprisonment for a term  not to exceed fifteen (15) days or both.  Section 7.   This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication in  accordance with law and approval by the New Jersey Department of Transportation.  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  Susan McCartney, Council President  Introduced: July 22, 2014  Adopted: August 12, 2014  Approved as to form on the basis of  the facts provided  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk

2414-14  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  The Ordinance, which amends the Traffic Regulations of the Township, designates the  intersections of Marshall Street and Phyllis Road and Holmes Street and Phyllis Road as Stop  Intersections with Stop Signs to be placed on Marshall Street and Holmes Street. These  intersections are   “ T ”   intersections with Marshall Street and Holmes Street forming the stems of  the   “ T ” . Due to volume of traffic which travels on Marshall Street and Phyllis Road and Holmes  Street and Phyllis Road the Township ’ s Engineering Division determined that traffic needed to  be regulated at these intersections for the safe movement of traffic through them. The  Engineering Division recommended designating the intersections as Stop Intersections with  Stop Signs on Marshall Street and on Holmes Street to provide for the safe movement of traffic  there. Without Stop Signs on Marshall Street and Holmes Street the normal right-of-way rule is  not expected to provide reasonably safe operation of these intersections.

2416-14  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This   Ordinance,   designates Glen View Drive between Northfield Avenue and Beacon  Place   as a   Restricted Parking Street on all days between the hours of 5:00 A.M. and 12:00  Midnight, and 12:00 Midnight and 2:00 A.M.   During these hours parking will only be allowed  by permit with permits issued only to residents of this section of Glen View Drive and their  guests. This section of Glen View Drive is used for parkin g   by bus commu ters, employees and  customers of   local businesses, and employees and visitors of local institutions   along Northfield  Avenue .   B etween the hours of 5:00 A.M. and 12:00 Midnight   and   12:00 Midni ght and 2:00 A.M.  this Ordinance   will curtail parking   on this section of Glen View Drive   by bus commu ters,  employees and customers of   local businesses, and employees and visitors of local institutions.  Parking between the hours of 2:00 A.M. and 5:00 A.M. is already prohibited by the overnight  parking prohibition regulation. This parking by permit regulation does not provide an exemption  from the overnight parking regulations.

2417-14  AN ORDINANCE   REQUIRING THE INSTALLATION OF A  KEY   LOCK   BOX EMERGENCY ACCESS SYSTEM FOR USE BY THE  WEST ORANGE   FIRE DEPARTMENT DURING AN EMERGENCY OR ANY OTHER  ACTION DEEMED NECESSARY BY THE   WEST ORANGE   FIRE DEPARTMENT  AND ADDING   SECTION   4   TO CHAPTER   18   OF THE  MUNICIPAL CODE OF THE   TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  WHEREAS,   the   health,   safety,   and   welfare   of   the   citizens   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange   ( “ Township ” )   are   promoted   by   requiring   certain   structures   to   have   a   key   lock   box  installed   on   the   exterior   of   the   structure   to   aid   the   Township   F ire   Department   in   gaining   acces s   to  the structure when responding to calls for emergency service; and  WHEREAS ,   many buildings are equipped with automatic systems that may cause the  Township   Fire   Department   to   be   summoned   at   a   time   of   emergency   when   the   building   or  business   is   not   occupied   or   when   the   occupant   is   not   available   to   provide   the   Township   Fire  Department entry into the building; and  WHEREAS,   the   key   lock   box   emergency   access   system   will   eliminate   costly   damage  from   forcible   entry   into   buildings   equipped   with   automatic   systems   by   the   Township   Fire  Department checking for fire or other dangerous conditions; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   Fire   Department   can   reduce   the   potential   for   rapid   expansion  of fire and other hazardous conditions by quick access and decreas e   dangers for firefighters; and  WHEREAS,   the   key   lock   box   emergency   access   system   has   been   adopted   by   Section  506   of the   International Fire Code 2006, New Jersey Edition ;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   key   lock   box   emergency   access   system   will   operate   on   a   controlled  master key basis by the   Township   Fire Department; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   is   authorized   to   pass   this   Ordinanc e   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.  40:48-2, and pursuant to   the Township ’ s   police powers.  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED ,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township  of   West   Orange   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   that   there   should   be   created  a   Chapter   18,   Section   4   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township,   entitled   “ Key   Lock

2  Box Emergency Access System, ”   which shall provide as follows:  18 - 4   KEY   LOCK   BOX EMERGENCY ACCESS SYSTEM  18-4.1   Definitions  Automatic   Dialer   shall   refer   to   a   device   that   automatically   dials   and   relays   a   prerecorded  message to a central station or the fire department.  Automatic   Fire   Suppression   System   refers   to   a   system   or   assembly   of   piping,   valves,   controls,  and   sprinklers,   which   are   designed   and   installed   to   comply   with   the   National   Fire   Protection  Association   (NFPA)   standards,   which   utilize   water,   foam,   carbon   dioxide,   or   other   gas   to  automatically react to suppress fire.  Fire Official   shall refer to the Fire Chief or his /her   designee.  Central   Station   shall   refer   to   an   office   of   a   private   company   to   which   remote   alarm   and  supervisory   signaling   devices   are   transmitted   and   where   personnel   are   in   attendance   at   all  times to supervise the circuits and investigate signals.  Key   Lock   Box   shall   refer   to   a   secure   rapid   entry   system   that   is   designed   to   be   used   by   Fire  Department   Personnel   in   the   event   of   an   emergency   to   gain   entry   into   a   struct ure   by   using   the  enclosed   owner- provided   key(s).   This   box   is   usually   mounted   on   the   exterior   of   the   building  in   a   location   that   is   specified   by   the   Fire   Official   and   Construction   Official .   All   boxes   shall  be   UL   (Underwriters   Laboratories)   certified ,   Knox   Box   brand,   and   approved   by   the   Fire  Official   and Construction Official .  Standpipe   System   shall   refer   to   a   wet   or   dry   system   of   piping,   valves,   outlets   and   related  equipment   designed   to   provide   water   at   specified   pressures   and   installed   exclusively   for   the  fighting of fires.  18- 4 . 2   Structures to Be Equipped with   a Key Lock Box  Unless   otherwise   determined   in   writing   by   the   Fire   Official,   t he   following   structures   shall   be  equipped   with   a   Key   Lock   Box   of   a   type   and   size   and   at   a   location   approved   by   the   Fire  Official   and Construction Official :  (a)   All   new   commercial   buildings   shall   be   equipped   with   a   Key   Lock   Box   prior   to   the  issuance of the   certificate   of occupancy.  (c)   All   existing   commercial   buil dings   constructing   additions,   major   renovations,   or  changes   of   use   tha t   require   Township   Zoning   or   Planning   Board   approval ,   shall  be equipped with   a   Key Lock Box   prior to the   issuance of any necessary   permit s .  (d)   All   existing   commercial   buildings   with   new   occupancies   by   tenants   shall   be  equipped   with   a   Key   Lock   Box   prior   to   the   issuance   of   a   Certificate   of   Continued  Occupancy.

3  (e)   Each   building   protected   by   an   automatic   fire   suppression   and/or   standpipe   system  and not manned 24 hour s a day, 7   day s a week .  (f)   Each   building   protected   by   an   automatic   fire   alarm   system   (automatic   dialer,  central   station,   external   audible/visual   alarm)   and   not   manned   24   hour s   a   day,   7  day s a week.  (g)   Multi-family   residential   structures   that   have   restricted   access   through   locked  doors   but   have   a   common   corridor   for   access   to   the   living   units ,   including   but   not  limited to condominium buildings.  (h)   Schools, whether public or private.  (i)   Governmental   structures   and   nursing   care   facilities ,   unless   the   building   is   staffed  or open   24 hour s a day, 7   day s a week.  18-4. 3   Key Lock Box Contents  The   property   owner   is   required   at   all   times   to   keep   a   key(s)   in   the   Key   Lock   Box   that   will  allow   access   to   the   structure.   The   Key   Lock   Box   shall   contain,   but   not   be   limited   to,   the  following items   as designated by the Fire Official:  (a)   Labeled   keys   to   locked   points   of   ingress   and   egress,   whether   in   interior   or   exterior   of  such   buildings;  (b)   Labeled keys to the locked mechanical rooms;  (c)   Labeled keys to locked elevator rooms and controls;  (d)   Labeled keys to any fence or secured areas;  (e)   Labeled   keys   to   areas   of   the   building   where   fire   alarm   panels   and   fire   protection  systems   are located;  (f)   Labeled keys to any other areas that may be required by the Fire Official;  (g)   A   card   containing   the   emergency   contact   names   and   p hone   numbers   for   such  buildings;  (h)   Floor plans of the rooms within the building showing   locations of shut- offs;  (i)   Hazardous materials information; and  (j)   An inventory of the keys.  The   property   owner   shall   notify   the   Township   Fire   Department   when   any   or   all   of   the   locks  or   keys   have   been   changed   and   shall   keep   the   immediate   area   of   the   Key   Lock   B ox   free   and  clear of any and all obstructions.

4  �-.4   Registration and   Installation of Key Lock Boxes  The   property   owner   shall   apply   for   a   registration   for   a   Key   Lock   B ox   on   forms   provided   by  and   obtained   from   the   Fire   Official .   A   registration   is   required   prior   to   the   installation   of   a  Key   Lock   B ox   in   order   to   verify   the   proper   size,   mounting   location   and   installation   of   said  Key   Lock   B ox.   The   Key   Lock   B ox   shall   be   install ed   at   a   location   approved   by   the   Fire  Official   and   the   Construction   Official .   No   Key   Lock   B ox   shall   be   installed,   voluntarily   or  otherwise,   without   first   obtaining   the   approval   of   the   Fire   Official   and   the   Construction  Official .   The   property   owner   shall   be   responsible   for   the   cost   to   purchase,   install,   and  maintain the   Key Lock B ox.  18-4. 5   Compliance  All   existing   buildings   required   to   have   a   Key   Lock   Box   shall   comply   with   this   Ordinance   one  (1)   yea r   from   its   effective   date.   All   newly   constructed   buildings   not   yet   occupied   or   buildings  currently   under   construction   and   all   buildings   or   businesses   applying   for   a   certificate   of  occupancy   shall   comply   immediately   upon   passage   of   this   Ordinance.   In   any   event,   the  owner   of   any   structure   in   the   Township   shall   comply   with   this   ordinance   within   sixty   (60)  days   from   receipt   of   written   notice   from   the   Fire   Official.   The   cost   of   purchasing   and  installing,   along   with   any   cost   associated   with   implementation   of   the   program   at   a   specific  property, will be   borne   by the   property owner .  18-4. 6   Penalties  Any   property   owner   failing   to   comply   with   this   Ordinance   shall   be   subject   to   a   fine   of   not  more than Three Hundred ($300) Dollars for each day of violation or non-compliance.  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor   Susan McCartney ,   Council President  K aren J. Carnevale ,   Municipal Clerk  Introduced: August 12, 2014  Adop t ed: September 9, 2014  2417-14

5  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  Authority   for   this   proposed   legislation   is   derived   from   N.J.S.A.   40:48-2,   entitling  municipalities   to   make   and   enforce   ordinances   and   regulations   it   deems   necessary   and   proper   for  the   protection   of   persons   and   property,   and   for   the   preservation   of   the   public   health,   safety   and  welfare   of   the   municipality   and   its   inhabitants.   T he   key   lock   box   emergency   access   system   has  also   been adopted by Section   506   of the   International Fire Co de 2006, New Jersey Edition.  The   purpose   of   the   proposed   legislation   is   to   promote   the   health,   safety,   and   welfare   of  the   Township ’ s   citizens   and   business   owners   by   allowing   the   Township   Fire   Department   to   gain  access   to   structures   when   same   is   not   occupied   or   when   the   occupants   are   unable   to   grant   ingress  to   the   Fire   Department.   The   improved   access   will   reduce   the   need   for   forced   entry   into  structures   and   should   avoid   costly   and   time-consuming   efforts   in   gaining   access   to   locked  structures during an emergency.  By   enabling   quick   access   to   buildings,   the   proposed   legislation   will   also   reduce   the   risk  of   rapid   extension   of   fire   and   other   hazardous   conditions .   Such   a   reduction   will   decrease   dangers  to firefighters ,   and   help minimize the   spread of property damage.  4837-2482-5626, v. 1

2418-14  AN ORDINANCE   REQUIRING THE INSTALLATION OF A   FIRE DEPARTMENT  CONNECTION INDICATOR LIGHT   FOR USE BY THE   WEST ORANGE   FIRE  DEPARTMENT AND ADDING   SECTION   5   TO CHAPTER   18   OF THE MUNICIPAL  CODE OF THE   TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  WHEREAS,   the   health,   safety,   and   welfare   of   the   citizens   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange   ( “ Township ” )   are   promoted   by   requiring   certain   structures   to   instal l   a   connection  indicator   light   on   the   exterior   of   the   structure   to   aid   the   Township   Fire   Department   in   locating  Fire Department Connections ( “ FDCs ” ) in emergency situations ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   Fire   Department   can   reduce   the   potential   for   rapid   extension  of   fire   and   other   hazardous   conditions   and   decreas e   dangers   for   firefighters   by   quick   access   to  FDCs ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   Construction   Official   and   Fire   Official   have   reviewed   this  ordinance   and   recommend its passage; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   is   authorized   to   pass   this   Ordinanc e   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.  40:48-2, and pursuant to   the Township ’ s   po lice powers .  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED ,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township  of   West   Orange   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   that   there   should   be   created  a   Chapter   18,   Section   5   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township,   entitled   “ Fire  Department Connection Indicator Light , ”   which shall provide as follows:  �-5   FIRE DEPARTMENT CONNECTION INDICATOR LIGHT  �-.1   The   owner   of   a   new   s tructure ,   an   ex isting   structure   undergoing   construction  requiring   a   new   or   revised   Certificate   of   Occupancy ,   or   an   existing   structure  undergoing   a   change   of   use,   having   a   sprinkler   and/or   standpipe   connection   either  attached   to   or   within   five   feet   of   said   structure ,   shall   be   required   to   install,   maintain  and   illuminate   a   weatherproof   light   fixture   containing   a   100   watt   light   source,   red   in  color,   mounted   on   the   structure   in   the   immediate   vicinity   of   the   sprinkler   or  standpipe   connection   at   a   height   of   seven   feet   above   the   connection.   The   exact   final

2  placement   and   design   of   the   light   fixture   shall   be   set   forth   on   plans   submitted   to   and  approved by the Fire   Chief   or his/ her designee   and the Construction Official .  �-.2   The   owner   of   a   new   s tructure,   an   existing   structure   undergoing   construction  requiring   a   new   or   revised   Certificate   of   Occupancy ,   or   an   existing   structure  undergoing   a   change   of   use,   having   a   sprinkler   and/or   standpipe   connection   remotely  located   at   a   distance   in   excess   of   five   feet   of   said   structure   shall   cause   to   be   placed,  installed,   maintained   and   illuminated   a   freestanding,   weatherproof   light   fixture  containing   a   100   watt   light   source,   red   in   color,   mounted   immediately   behind   the  fixture   at   a   height   of   four   feet.   The   exact   final   placement   and   design   of   the   light  fixture   shall   be   set   forth   on   plans   submitted   to   and   approved   by   the   Fire   Chief   or  his/ her designee   and the Construction Official .  �-.3   In   certain   cases,   based   upon   extraordinary   condition s ,   the   Fire   C hief,   or   his/ her  designee,   shall   have   the   right   to   grant   a   waiver   of   the   requirements   imposed   by   this  section,   on   an   individual   basis;   however,   some   form   of   other   substitute   identification  will be required.  �-.4   The   owner   of   any   structure   in   the   Township   which   falls   within   the   requirements   of  this   action   shall   cause   the   structure   to   be   brought   into   compliance   with   this   Section  within   one   (1)   year   from   its   effective   date,   or   60   days   from   receipt   of   written   notice  from the Fire   Chief, or his / her designee , whichever is sooner.  �-.5   Any   property   owner   failing   to   comply   with   this   Ordinance   shall   be   subject   to   a   fine  of   not   more   than   Three   Hundred   ($300)   Dollars   for   each   day   of   violation   or   non-  compliance.  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor   Susan McCartney, Council President  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk  Introduced: August 12, 2014  Adop t ed: September 9, 2014

3  2418-14  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  Authority   for   this   proposed   legislation   is   derived   from   N.J.S.A.   40:48-2,   entitling  municipalities   to   make   and   enforce   ordinances   and   regulations   it   deems   necessary   and   proper   for  the   protection   of   persons   and   property,   and   for   the   preservation   of   the   public   health,   safety   and  welfare of the municipality and its inhabitants.  The   purpose   of   the   proposed   legislation   is   to   promote   the   health,   safety,   and   welfare   of  the   Township ’ s   citizens   and   business   owners   by   allowing   the   Township   Fire   Department   to  locate   and   gain   access   to   Fire   Department   Connections   ( “ FDCs ” )   as   quickly   as   possible   during  emergency   situations.   FDC s   are   intended   to   provide   sprinkler   systems   with   a   means   to   receive  water   support   from   a   Fire   D epartment   pumping   apparatus.   This   ability   is   necessary   when  sprinkler   systems   need   supplemental   water   support,   and   when   e vents   such   as   floods,   hurricanes,  and   similar   events   interrupt   utility-provided   water   because   of   loss   of   power   to   pumping   stations  or   damage   to   the   water   supply   infrastructure   piping.   If   these   events   were   to   interrupt   the  automatic   water   supply   to   a   sprinkler   system,   then   the   only   possibility   of   providing   water   to   the  sprinkler   would   be   through   the   FDC.   By   enabling   quick   access   to   FDCs ,   the   proposed  legislation   will   reduce   the   risk   of   rapid   extension   of   fire   and   other   hazardous   conditions .   Such   a  reduction   will decrease dangers to firefighters ,   and   help minimize the   spread of property damage.  4833-4677-0202, v. 1

2419-14  AN ORDINANCE   AMENDING   CHAPTER   4, SECTION 35.2   OF THE REVISED  GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  I.   PURPOSE  WHEREAS ,   the   existence   of   stray,   feral   and   otherwise   unconfined   wildlife   in   the  Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   creates   a   hazard   to   the   health   of   Township  residents and their pets; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township ’ s   Health   Department   (the   “ Department ” )   reports   that   during  the   period   between   2010   and   2013,   approximately   four-hundred   and   thirty   (430)   stray   cat  complaints   were   registered   with   the   Department ’ s   Animal   Control   Division   ( “ ACD ” ),   and   five-  hundred and seventeen (517) stray cats were impounded ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Department   reports   that   during   the   period   between   January   1,   2014   and  June   30,   2014,   sixty-eight   (68)   stray   cat   complaints   were   registered   with   the   ACD,   and   eighty-  one (81) stray cats were impounded; and  WHEREAS,   the   intentional   feeding   of   stray,   feral   and   otherwise   unconfined   wildlife  within   the   Township   is   a   major   cause   of   the   proliferation   of   stray,   feral   and   otherwise   unconfined  wildlife in the Township;   and  WHEREAS,   Chapter   4,   Section   35.2   of   the   Township ’ s   Revised   General   Ordinances  ( “ Section   35.2 ” )   prohibits   the   feeding   of   wildlife,   excluding   confined   wildlife,   in   any   public   park   or  Township-owned property; and  WHEREAS,   the   prohibition   set   forth   in   Section   35.2,   insofar   as   it   is   limited   in   application  to   public   parks   and   Township-owned   property,   has   proven   insufficient   to   prevent   the  aforementioned hazard to the health of Township residents and their pets;

2  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New   Jersey,   that  Chapter   4 ,   Section   35.2   the   Revised   General   Ordinance s   of   the   Township   shall   be   amended   as  follows:  II.   CHAPTER   4,   SECTION   35.2   SHALL   BE   AND   HEREBY   IS   AMENDED   AND  SUPERSEDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS:  4-35.2   Prohibited Conduct  No   person   shall   feed   any   wildlife,   excluding   confined   wildlife,   anywhere   within   the  Township.  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED,   that   if   any   section,   paragraph,   subsection,   clause   or  provision   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   adjudged   by   the   courts   to   be   invalid,   such   adjudication   shall  apply   only   to   the   section,   paragraph,   subsection,   clause   or   provision   so   adjudicated,   and   the  remainder of the Ordinance shall be deemed valid and effective.  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED ,   that   any   ordinances   or   parts   thereof   in   conflict   with   the  provisions of this Ordinance are repealed to the extent of such conflict.  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED ,   that   this   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   passage   and  publication in accordance with law.  Susan McCartney   Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  Council President  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk  Introduction :   August 12, 2014  Adopted: September 9, 2014  4826-2165-6348, v. 1

2420-14  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance ,   to improve both vehicular and pedestrian safety in the vicinity of the  Hazel Avenue School ,   will prohibit certain turning movements on Hazel Avenue from  intersecting streets and a school driveway on school days during the hours of 7:30 A.M. to 9:00  A.M. and 2:30 P.M. to 4:00 P.M.   T hese proposed regulations will prohibit turning movements in  an easterly direction on Hazel Avenue from Oak Ridge   R oad, Birch Street and the easterly  school driveway. This will minimize conflicts between vehicles traveling   i n opposite directions  on Hazel Avenue and keep most traffic flowing westerly on Hazel Avenue with the school  located to the right on the northerly side of the street.

2422-14  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  To   provide   additional   on   street   parking   in   the   Valley   section   of   West  Orange,   this   Ordinance   will   rescind   the   on   street   parking   prohibition   on   the  easterly   side   of   Rollinson   Street   between   Pillot   Place   and   Swaine   Place.   Parking  will   still   be   prohibited   at   all   times   on   that   side   between   Mitchell   Street   and   Pillot  Place   and   the   one   block   from   Swaine   Place   to   Northfield   Avenue.   However ,   to  allow   for   street   cleaning   and   maintenance ,   parking   on   the   east erly   side   of   Rollinson  Street   between   Pillot   Place   and   Swaine   Place   will   be   prohibited   on   Mondays  between   the   hours   of   8:00   A.M.   and   10:00   A.M.   Finally   overnight   parking   is  allowed   on   Rollinson   Street   where   parking   is   allowed   and   this   Ordinance   addresses  that   by   amending   Revised   General   Ordinance   7-14.1c   which   lists   certain  exceptions to the overnight prohibition.
